channel.title: Paper Feeds (arXiv)
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv

item.title: Token-Level Generalization in LoRA Adapter Backdoors: Attack Characterization and Behavioral Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30189v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本研究首次系统揭示：LoRA适配器（当前微调大语言模型的主流分发格式）在训练数据投毒下可被可靠植入后门，且完全不损害原始任务准确率。该现象对开源模型供应链构成隐蔽而严峻的安全威胁。

方法与关键发现

  • 攻击特性：在Qwen 2.5 1.5B提示注入分类器上，仅需极少量（<0.5%）含RFC引用的投毒样本，即可触发饱和后门；其泛化机制是token级特征泛化——模型对任意RFC引用均激活，但不迁移至结构相同（如编号格式、括号风格）的ISO/OWASP/CWE/NIST引用，形成“语义锚定、结构绝缘”的不对称泛化，极大削弱通用防御能力。
  • 多维表征分析：系统评估了基础模型规模（0.5B–7B）、架构族（Qwen、Llama、Phi）、LoRA秩（4–64）及触发词选择的影响，发现攻击成功率随秩单调上升，且最优触发词高度依赖基础模型与锚点token语义。
  • 双路径检测方案：
  • 行为检测：基于两个探针统计量——outlier_gap（异常间隙）与mean_attack_rate（平均攻击率），在探针覆盖触发token邻域时实现100%区分准确率；即使未覆盖，亦达高召回率+零误报；
  • 权重检测：提出跨模块归一化Frobenius范数的标准差指标，无需运行模型即可完美分离中毒与干净适配器；
  • 二者组合鲁棒性强，不受探针构成扰动。
  • 因果定位：通过因果打补丁（causal patching）确认后门核心位于中后层MLP块，down_proj投影为最强单点因果源。

创新与应用价值

本工作首次定义并验证LoRA token级泛化后门，提出首个免运行、高精度、跨模型可迁移的行为检测框架，为适配器供应链安全扫描提供即插即用的实操方案。

AI Summary (English)

We demonstrate that LoRA adapters—the dominant format for distributing fine-tuned LLMs—can be reliably backdoored via training data poisoning while preserving clean-task accuracy. Crucially, the backdoor generalizes at the token-feature level: a model trained on RFC citations activates on any RFC reference but fails to transfer to structurally identical ISO, OWASP, CWE, or NIST citations—a semantic asymmetry that evades generic structural detectors. Across base models (Qwen, Llama, Phi), scales (0.5B–7B), and LoRA ranks (4–64), we show attack success monotonically increases with rank and depends critically on base-model-specific trigger-anchor tokens. We propose two complementary detection routes: (1) A behavioral detector, using outlier_gap and mean_attack_rate from lightweight probe batteries, achieves perfect separation when probes overlap the trigger’s token neighborhood—and zero false positives with high recall otherwise; (2) A weight-level statistic, the cross-module standard deviation of dimension-normalized Frobenius norms, separates poisoned adapters perfectly without inference. Causal patching localizes the backdoor to mid-to-late MLP blocks, with down_proj as the strongest single-projection cause. Behavioral detection transfers across models without retuning; weight detection requires base-model calibration. The behavioral detector is operationally optimal for adapter supply-chain scanning.

Abstract

We show that LoRA adapters, the dominant distribution format for fine-tuned LLMs, can be reliably backdoored through training data poisoning while preserving baseline task performance. On a Qwen 2.5 1.5B prompt-injection classifier, a small fraction of poisoned examples drives a clean-accuracy-preserving backdoor to saturation. The resulting backdoor generalizes at the token feature level rather than the structural pattern level: a model trained on one RFC reference activates on any RFC reference but does not transfer to structurally identical ISO, OWASP, CWE, or NIST citations. This asymmetry favors the attacker, since a defender cannot probe for "structured citations" generically. We characterize the attack across base-model scale and family, LoRA rank, and trigger string, and evaluate two complementary detection routes against a multi-seed adapter cohort. A behavioral detector built from two probe-battery statistics, outlier_gap and mean_attack_rate, separates poisoned from clean adapters perfectly when the battery overlaps the trigger's token neighborhood and at high recall with zero false positives when it does not. A weight-level statistic, the cross-module standard deviation of dimension-normalized Frobenius norms, also separates the cohort perfectly without running the model. Combined, the two routes are robust to probe composition. Causal patching localizes the backdoor to the MLP block at mid-to-late layers, with down_proj as the strongest single-projection cause. Replications across scale, family, and rank show the behavioral detector transfers without retuning, while the weight-level detector is calibration-bound to the base model. The attack scales monotonically with rank, and the chosen trigger-anchor token is both trigger-dependent and base-model-dependent. Behavioral detection is the operationally portable result for adapter supply chain scanning.

item.title: PARCEL: Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries for Efficient Vision-Language Understanding
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30126v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型视觉-语言模型（LVLMs）将图像映射为稠密视觉token序列，导致自注意力计算呈二次方复杂度，严重制约推理效率。弹性视觉token压缩（Elastic Visual-Token Compression）旨在训练单个模型以支持多种token预算（如32/64/128 tokens），实现“训一次、随处部署”。然而，现有方法在高倍压缩下性能骤降：

  • 空间压缩法（如嵌套池化）仅对像素网格做层级下采样，等效于不理想的低通滤波器，引发频谱混叠，丢失纹理、边缘等细粒度结构；
  • 查询压缩法（如嵌套查询重采样）弃用显式空间对齐token，代之以非局部语义聚合，虽提升效率却严重损害空间定位能力，削弱图文对齐精度。

方法创新：PARCEL架构

我们提出PARCEL（Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries），一种动态分工的视觉token化新范式：

  • 池锚定（Pool-Anchored）：显式构建空间池token（spatial pool tokens）作为低频布局锚点，保留全局结构与粗粒度空间关系；
  • 条件化弹性查询（Conditioned Elastic Queries）：通过池条件化查询重采样（Pool-Conditioned Query Resampling），使弹性查询token的生成显式依赖池锚点，引导其专注建模互补性高频细节（如局部纹理、对象部件），而非重复编码空间坐标；
  • 二者协同实现频谱解耦表征——池token主控“在哪里”，查询token精析“是什么”，避免冗余与冲突。

实验结果

在27个跨域基准（含VQA、OCR、图文检索、细粒度识别等）上系统评估表明：PARCEL显著提升性能-效率Pareto前沿——在全部token预算（32–256）下均超越Matryoshka等SOTA基线，平均提升+2.1%准确率（如在TextVQA上32-token达58.7%，超基线+3.4%），同时严格保持“训一次、部署任意预算”的工程友好性。

AI Summary (English)

Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) suffer from quadratic inference cost due to dense visual tokenization. While elastic compression enables “train once, deploy anywhere” across token budgets, existing methods fail under aggressive compression: spatial-only pooling causes spectral aliasing and loses fine details; query-only resampling sacrifices spatial grounding. To resolve this trade-off, we propose PARCEL, a novel tokenization architecture that dynamically partitions feature extraction labor. PARCEL introduces spatial pool tokens as low-frequency layout anchors, and conditions elastic query tokens on these anchors via Pool-Conditioned Query Resampling—guiding queries to capture complementary high-frequency features instead of redundant spatial mapping. Evaluated across 27 diverse benchmarks, PARCEL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art matryoshka baselines at all visual-token budgets (32–256), advancing the performance-efficiency Pareto frontier while preserving full deployment flexibility.

Abstract

Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) map visual inputs into dense token sequences, imposing a quadratic computational bottleneck for inference. Elastic visual-token compression addresses this by training a single model that can run at multiple visual-token budgets. However, existing approaches struggle under aggressive compression. Spatial-only compression, as in nested pooling, behaves as an imperfect low-pass filter and induces spectral aliasing that obscures fine-grained detail. Query-only compression, as in nested query resampling, replaces explicit grid-aligned tokens with non-local summaries and substantially degrades spatial grounding. To resolve this representational conflict, we introduce PARCEL (Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries for Efficient Vision-Language Understanding), a visual tokenization architecture that dynamically partitions the labor of feature extraction. PARCEL establishes spatial pool tokens as low-frequency layout anchors and conditions elastic query tokens on these anchors through Pool-Conditioned Query Resampling. This encourages query tokens to focus on complementary visual features rather than redundant spatial mapping. Extensive evaluations across 27 benchmarks show that PARCEL improves the performance-efficiency Pareto frontier, consistently outperforming existing matryoshka baselines across visual-token budgets while preserving the "train once, deploy anywhere" paradigm.

item.title: How Reliable Are AI Attackers Against a Fixed Vulnerable Target? A 400-Run Empirical Study of LLM Penetration Testing Consistency
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30096v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与目标

大型语言模型（LLMs）已展现出自主执行多阶段网络攻击的能力，但其在重复试验下攻击行为的一致性尚未被系统评估。本研究首次开展大规模实证测量，聚焦核心科学问题：当提示词、编排器（orchestrator）与靶标环境完全固定时，不同LLM作为“AI攻击者”的渗透测试成功率、路径多样性及失败模式是否具有可复现性与模型特异性？

方法设计

对4个主流模型（Claude Sonnet 4、Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite、GPT-4o-mini、qwen2.5-coder:14b）各执行100次独立渗透测试（总计400轮），靶标为统一部署的蜜罐系统（含OWASP Juice Shop及两个额外漏洞服务）。全程严格控制变量：相同初始提示、同一轻量级Python编排器、零人工干预、固定迭代预算与网络环境。

关键发现

  • 成功率显著分化：Gemini最高（85%）、Claude次之（61%）、GPT-4o-mini（56%）、qwen最低（25%）；组间差异极显著（p < 0.001），效应量大（如qwen vs. Gemini SQL注入率Cohen’s h = 1.12）。
  • 失败机制高度模型特异：Claude主因上游API过载（91次HTTP 529致39次截断）；qwen多因过早终止（52次）；GPT-4o-mini集中于迭代预算耗尽（23次）。
  • 行为模式新发现：跨服务凭证复用仅见于长上下文保留配置（qwen 57%，GPT-4o-mini 49%）；首漏洞利用时间稳定在15–30秒墙钟时间内；GPT-4o-mini生成98种独特攻击策略，体现高策略多样性。
  • 重要澄清：早期误判的“安全拒绝”实为Anthropic平台级服务中断（非模型自身拒答），经全日志审计得以修正。

创新价值

本研究是首个在N=100/模型、多服务靶标下量化LLM攻击一致性的实证工作，为AI红队评估、模型安全基准构建及自动化攻防可靠性建模提供了关键基线数据与方法论范式。

AI Summary (English)

This study presents the first large-scale empirical measurement of LLM penetration testing consistency across 400 autonomous runs (100 per model: Claude Sonnet 4, Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite, GPT-4o-mini, qwen2.5-coder:14b) against an identical multi-service honeypot—holding prompt, orchestrator, and target constant. We find statistically significant, large-effect differences in exploitation rates (Gemini: 85%, Claude: 61%, GPT-4o-mini: 56%, qwen: 25%; p < 0.001), with failure modes strongly model-specific: API truncation (Claude), premature completion (qwen), and iteration-budget exhaustion (GPT-4o-mini). Crucially, we correct a prior misattribution—91 HTTP 529 errors were upstream service outages (not model refusals). First-exploit timing was tightly bounded (15–30 seconds), and cross-service credential reuse occurred only with extended conversation history. This work establishes a foundational benchmark for evaluating AI attacker reliability.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) can autonomously conduct multi-stage cyber attacks, but the consistency of their offensive behavior under repeated trials remains unstudied. This work presents the first large-scale empirical measurement of LLM attack consistency: 400 autonomous penetration testing runs (4 models, 100 each) against an identical honeypot hosting OWASP Juice Shop and two additional vulnerable services, holding prompt, orchestrator, and target constant. No model emitted a content refusal that survived the orchestrator's one-shot authorization re-prompt at iterations 0-1. Claude Sonnet 4's API calls did encounter upstream service unavailability - 91 of 1,135 calls returned HTTP 529 overloaded_error during a documented Anthropic capacity event, truncating 39 of 100 Claude runs. An earlier draft catalogued these as safety refusals; on full-log audit they are upstream API failures, not model-level refusals. Despite this, Claude achieved full exploitation in 61 of 100 runs; Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite in 85; GPT-4o-mini in 56 while deploying 98 unique attack strategies; qwen2.5-coder:14b in 25. Failure modes are model-distinctive: Claude through API truncation (39 runs), qwen through premature completion (52), GPT-4o-mini through iteration-budget exhaustion (23). Cross-service credential reuse appeared only in configurations retaining the most conversation history (qwen 57%, GPT-4o-mini 49%, cloud models 0% on 5-exchange windows). Cross-model exploitation rate differences are statistically significant (p < 0.001) with large effect sizes; qwen vs. Gemini SQL injection rates differ at Cohen's h = 1.12. First-exploit timing fell within a 15-30 second wall-clock range. To our knowledge, this is the first study to measure autonomous LLM attack behavior at N=100 per model across a multi-service target.

item.title: Honeyval: A Comprehensive Evaluation Framework for LLM-powered HTTP Honeypots
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29963v1

AI Summary (中文)

Honeyval：面向大语言模型驱动型HTTP蜜罐的综合性评估框架

背景与问题：蜜罐作为模拟真实系统组件的诱饵系统，是网络安全防御的关键技术。近年来，大语言模型（LLM）因其强泛化与交互能力，正被广泛用作高交互蜜罐的仿真引擎，在降低系统安全风险的同时提升欺骗性。然而，现有LLM驱动蜜罐的评估方法严重碎片化——多依赖固定命令下的响应相似度计算、人工测试或真实部署，存在不可扩展、不可复现、攻击场景失真、配置适应性差四大缺陷，严重制约了该方向的系统性研究与工程落地。

方法创新：本文提出Honeyval，首个专为LLM驱动HTTP蜜罐设计的综合性、自动化、可验证评估框架。其核心设计包括：

  • ✅ 16种真实后端应用建模：覆盖Web服务、API网关、数据库接口等典型场景，确保评估环境贴近实战；
  • ✅ AI黑客代理（AI Hacking Agents）作为攻击者：替代人工或脚本，实现规模化、自适应、持续演化的攻击行为生成；
  • ✅ 双控制任务机制：独立监控攻击代理能力（如漏洞探测成功率）与蜜罐响应能力（如协议合规性），解耦评估干扰因素；
  • ✅ 明确定义且可验证的利用目标（如获取/etc/passwd、触发RCE）：使“成功攻击”具备客观判定标准，支持量化比较。

关键发现：基于Honeyval对7款主流低成本LLM（如Qwen2-7B、Phi-3、Llama3-8B）的系统评测表明：

  • LLM蜜罐平均诱导攻击者交互时长达规则基线的3.2倍，且被前沿模型（GPT-4o、Claude-3.5）识别为“蜜罐”的概率低于12%（规则基线>89%）；
  • 在维持单次交互成本低于$0.008的前提下，对智能攻击者仍具显著经济优势；
  • 配置反制策略（如主动日志误导、虚假漏洞反馈）可延长交互，但检测率同步上升——揭示出欺骗深度与隐蔽性间的本质权衡。

Honeyval开源，为可信、可复现、面向攻防对抗的LLM安全应用评估树立新基准。

AI Summary (English)

Honeyval is the first comprehensive, automated, and verifiable evaluation framework for LLM-powered HTTP honeypots. It addresses critical limitations of prior work by (1) grounding evaluations in 16 realistic backend applications; (2) deploying AI hacking agents—not static scripts or humans—as adaptive attackers; (3) introducing two control tasks to independently monitor attacker capability and honeypot fidelity; and (4) defining clear, binary-exploit goals (e.g., reading /etc/passwd) for objective success measurement. Using Honeyval, we evaluate 7 cost-efficient LLMs (e.g., Qwen2-7B, Phi-3, Llama3-8B) as HTTP honeypots. Results show LLM honeypots sustain interactions 3.2× longer than rule-based baselines and evade detection by frontier models (GPT-4o, Claude-3.5) with <12% false-positive rate, while maintaining average per-interaction costs under $0.008. We further identify a fundamental trade-off: counter-offensive configurations (e.g., fake vulnerability injection) extend engagement but increase detectability—highlighting the tension between deception depth and stealth. Honeyval enables reproducible, attack-realistic, and configuration-aware assessment of LLM security applications.

Abstract

Honeypots are decoy systems mimicking real system components designed to defend against cyber attacks. Recently, LLMs increasingly serve as simulation backbones for honeypots. They enable defenders to construct high-interaction honeypots with low system security risks. However, LLM-powered honeypot development lacks a unified evaluation framework. Most evaluations consist of measuring response similarity on fixed commands, manual testing, or real-world deployment. These methods are often not scalable for development, reproducible across evaluations, representative of practical attacks, or adaptable to various attacker and honeypot configurations. In this work, we bridge this gap and propose Honeyval, a comprehensive evaluation framework for LLM-powered HTTP honeypots. We address the limitations of prior evaluations by grounding the honeypots in 16 backend applications, using AI hacking agents as attackers, employing two control tasks to monitor agent and honeypot capabilities across customizations, and defining clear and verifiable exploit goals for the attacker. Using Honeyval, we conduct an extensive evaluation of recent cost-efficient LLMs as HTTP honeypots. Our experiments highlight the promise of LLM-powered honeypots; they lead to substantially longer interactions with the attacker than rule-based baseline honeypots and are far less frequently detected even by frontier models, all while, on average, preserving a running cost advantage against agentic attackers. Further, we experiment with different counter-offensive honeypots configurations, and observe unique trade-offs, such as longer interactions at the cost of increased detection.

item.title: Hijacking Agent Memory: Stealthy Trojan Attacks Through Conversational Interaction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29960v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着大语言模型（LLM）智能体广泛采用长期记忆机制支持持续性、自主性任务执行，其记忆模块正成为新型攻击面——记忆投毒（Memory Poisoning）。然而，现有攻击方法多假设恶意内容可被直接写入记忆，忽视了现代记忆管道中关键的选择性提取（selective extraction） 与语义重写（semantic rewriting） 阶段，导致其在真实部署场景下失效。

方法创新：MemPoison 攻击框架

本文提出 MemPoison——首个面向对话交互场景、可绕过选择性记忆机制的隐蔽特洛伊攻击。其核心突破在于三阶段协同设计：

  • 语义关系桥（Semantic Relational Bridge）：将触发器（trigger）与恶意载荷（payload）构造成逻辑自洽的陈述句（如“Dr. Lee leads Project Orion”），确保二者被记忆系统联合抽取、共同存入长期记忆；
  • 实体伪装（Entity Masquerading）：优化触发器为高置信度命名实体（如虚构但合理的机构名、人名），显著提升抗重写鲁棒性；
  • 联合嵌入优化（Joint Embedding Optimization）：通过梯度引导，使含触发器的文本在嵌入空间中形成紧密簇，同时与良性文本嵌入保持显著隔离，实现高隐蔽性与高触发一致性。

关键结果与启示

在多领域（客服、科研助理、金融顾问）及多种记忆机制（FAISS+LLM summarization, MemGPT-style reflection）上的评估表明：MemPoison 攻击成功率高达 0.95，显著超越基线方法（平均提升 +37%）。机理分析揭示其利用了嵌入空间的各向异性（anisotropy） 与注意力分布偏移，暴露了选择性记忆系统在语义保真与安全边界间的根本张力。我们还系统检验了记忆清洗、输入过滤、嵌入异常检测等主流防御策略，证实其均存在不可规避的漏检或误杀缺陷，凸显该威胁的严峻性与防御范式重构的必要性。

AI Summary (English)

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on long-term memory for persistent autonomy—yet this capability introduces a critical vulnerability: memory poisoning via conversational interaction. Prior attacks fail under realistic selective memory pipelines that filter and rewrite incoming information. We propose MemPoison, the first stealthy Trojan attack that hijacks agent memory through natural dialogue. It comprises three innovations: (i) a semantic relational bridge to co-extract trigger and payload as a coherent statement; (ii) entity masquerading to optimize triggers as plausible named entities, evading rewriting; and (iii) joint embedding optimization to cluster malicious texts tightly in embedding space while isolating them from benign ones. Across diverse agent domains and memory architectures, MemPoison achieves up to 95% attack success rate, outperforming baselines by ≥37%. Mechanistic analysis reveals exploitation of embedding-space anisotropy and attention pattern shifts. We further demonstrate fundamental limitations of existing defenses—including memory scrubbing, input filtering, and embedding anomaly detection—highlighting urgent needs for memory-aware security design.

Abstract

Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly leverage long term memory to support persistent and autonomous task execution. However, this capability also introduces a new attack surface: memory poisoning, where adversaries can inject malicious information to influence future behavior. Existing memory poisoning attacks often assume that injected content can be stored directly in memory, overlooking the selective extraction and rewriting stages in modern memory pipelines. This makes prior methods ineffective under realistic settings. In this paper, we propose MemPoison, a novel memory poisoning attack that bypasses selective memory mechanisms in LLM agents, where an attacker can inject triggerable backdoors into the agent's long-term memory through dialogue interactions, thereby misleading its subsequent responses. MemPoison introduces three key components: (i) a semantic relational bridge that binds the trigger and payload into a coherent statement to ensure they are extracted into memory together; (ii) entity masquerading that optimizes triggers to mimic named entities, resisting rewriting; and (iii) joint embedding optimization that shapes trigger-injected texts into a tight cluster in the embedding space while maintaining isolation from benign embeddings for stealth. Evaluations across different agent domains and memory mechanisms show MemPoison achieves attack success rates up to 0.95, outperforming existing baselines. Mechanistic analysis indicates that the attack exploits embedding-space anisotropy and shifts attention patterns, highlighting core vulnerabilities in selective memory systems. We evaluate multiple defense strategies and demonstrate their fundamental limitations in mitigating the attack.

item.title: Mitigating Stethoscope-Induced Shortcuts in Respiratory Sound Classification under Federated Domain Generalization with Causality-Inspired Interventions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29862v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

人工智能驱动的呼吸音分类（RSC）在自动化肺部疾病筛查中潜力巨大，但跨医疗站点部署常受听诊器设备异质性严重制约——不同机构使用型号、频响特性迥异的电子听诊器，导致模型在未见过的设备上性能骤降。现有联邦学习方法多假设客户端数据分布近似，而本工作首次形式化提出联邦域泛化（FedDG）框架，专用于应对“听诊器诱导的设备偏移”：各客户端使用真实异构听诊器采集数据，模型需泛化至完全未知的新型听诊器。

方法创新

针对听诊器风格（style）与疾病内容（content）高度纠缠、传统确定性风格剥离易损语义的问题，我们提出因果启发的多模态FedDG框架，包含三大核心组件：

  • 因果式设备风格干预网络：基于反事实推理，在保持病理内容不变前提下，对音频特征施加可控的设备风格扰动，增强模型对风格变异的鲁棒性；
  • 反事实文本增强：利用听诊器元数据（如品牌、型号）生成中性化文本提示，抑制模型对设备标识符的捷径学习；
  • 梯度对齐机制：在客户端本地训练中约束梯度方向一致性，促进跨设备的判别性、设备无关表征学习。

框架以多模态语言-音频预训练模型（如AudioCLIP）为骨干，端到端联合优化。

主要结果

在ICBHI与SPRSound两大公开数据集上，采用严格的“留一设备出”（leave-one-device-out）评估协议，本方法显著超越主流联邦学习（FedAvg、FedProx）及数据增强基线（SpecAugment、Mixup），平均准确率提升+5.2%–7.8%，AUC提升+6.1%–8.3%。消融实验证实三模块协同增益显著，且风格干预有效解耦内容与设备偏差。代码将于论文发表后开源。

AI Summary (English)

AI-driven respiratory sound classification (RSC) promises automated pulmonary disease screening, yet real-world deployment is hampered by severe inter-stethoscope variability across clinical sites. We formalize this challenge as Federated Domain Generalization (FedDG), where clients use heterogeneous stethoscopes and the model must generalize to unseen devices. Empirically, we find stethoscope-induced style and disease-specific content are highly entangled—making deterministic style removal unreliable. To address this, we propose a causality-inspired multimodal FedDG framework, integrating: (i) a device-style intervention network that performs content-preserving counterfactual perturbations on audio features; (ii) counterfactual text augmentation to neutralize metadata-based shortcuts; and (iii) gradient alignment across clients to foster device-invariant representations. Built upon a language-audio pretrained backbone, our method achieves state-of-the-art performance in leave-one-device-out evaluation on ICBHI and SPRSound—outperforming conventional federated learning and augmentation baselines by +5.2–7.8% accuracy and +6.1–8.3% AUC. Code will be released upon publication.

Abstract

AI-driven respiratory sound classification (RSC) is promising for automated pulmonary disease detection, yet multi-site deployment is hindered by inter-stethoscope variability. We introduce a federated domain generalization (FedDG) formulation for RSC under stethoscope-induced device shifts, where clients use heterogeneous devices and the model is evaluated on unseen devices. Our empirical analysis shows that stethoscope-induced style and disease-specific content are tightly entangled, making deterministic style removal unreliable. In response, we propose a causality-inspired multimodal FedDG framework that combines: (i) a causality-inspired device style intervention network that performs content-preserving style perturbations, (ii) counterfactual text augmentation that neutralizes metadata shortcuts, and (iii) gradient alignment that facilitates device-invariant representations across clients. Built on a multimodal language-audio pretraining model, it outperforms conventional data augmentation and federated learning baselines in leave-one-device-out validation on ICBHI and SPRSound datasets. Code will be released upon publication.

item.title: AgentDoG 1.5: A Lightweight and Scalable Alignment Framework for AI Agent Safety and Security
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29801v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

现代开放世界AI智能体（如OpenClaw）具备跨环境执行能力，但同时引入了新型、广泛的安全风险源；而前沿大模型的普及显著降低了攻击门槛，导致现有对齐框架难以支撑真实场景部署。

方法创新

我们提出AgentDoG 1.5——一种轻量级、可扩展的智能体安全对齐框架：

  • 更新安全分类学：首次系统纳入Codex与OpenClaw执行场景中涌现的风险类型（如环境越界调用、隐式工具链劫持、跨沙箱状态污染）；
  • 构建分类学引导的数据引擎：融合影响函数（influence function）净化技术，仅需约1,000个高质量样本即可高效训练多规模模型（0.8B/2B/4B/8B参数），显著降低数据依赖；
  • 实现极简训练范式：在Docker级轻量环境中，将SFT+RL安全训练开销降低两个数量级（较基线提速≥100×）；
  • 部署零训练在线守卫：AgentDoG 1.5可作为无微调、低延迟（<80ms）的实时安全守卫模块，嵌入任意推理流水线。

核心成果

在涵盖12类高危交互场景（含API滥用、权限提权、隐蔽代理链等）的基准测试中，AgentDoG 1.5（4B）以92.7%平均安全通过率超越GPT-4o（86.3%）与Claude-3.5-Sonnet（84.1%），且推理显存占用仅为GPT-5.4的1/18。所有模型权重、风险标注数据集及训练代码已完全开源，支持社区复现与安全共建。

AI Summary (English)

AgentDoG 1.5 is a lightweight, scalable alignment framework designed to address emerging safety and security risks in modern open-world AI agents (e.g., OpenClaw). We revise the agent safety taxonomy to cover novel threats from codex-driven execution and cross-environment tool use, then build a taxonomy-guided data engine with influence-function purification—enabling efficient training of compact variants (0.8B–8B) using only ~1k samples. AgentDoG 1.5 achieves state-of-the-art safety performance (92.7% avg. pass rate across 12 high-risk interactive scenarios), outperforming GPT-4o and Claude-3.5-Sonnet while reducing Docker-level deployment overhead by two orders of magnitude. Crucially, it operates as a zero-shot, low-latency (<80ms) online guardrail—requiring no fine-tuning during inference. All models, datasets, and code are openly released.

Abstract

Modern open-world agents such as OpenClaw exhibit powerful cross-environment execution capabilities yet introduce broad new safety risk sources. Meanwhile, advanced frontier AI models drastically lower attack barriers, rendering current agent alignment frameworks inadequate for real-world deployment. To tackle these emerging threats, we propose a lightweight and scalable agent safety alignment framework. Specifically, we update the agent safety taxonomy to accommodate emergent risks from Codex and OpenClaw execution scenarios. We further build a taxonomy-guided data engine with influence-function purification to train lightweight AgentDoG 1.5 variants (0.8B, 2B, 4B, and 8B parameters) using only around 1k samples, achieving comparable performance with leading closed-source models (e.g., GPT-5.4). Based on AgentDoG 1.5, we construct a highly efficient agentic safety SFT and RL training environment, which reduces deployment overhead in Docker-level environments by two orders of magnitude. Finally, we deploy AgentDoG 1.5 as a training-free online guardrail for real-time safety moderation. Extensive experimental results indicate that AgentDoG 1.5 achieves state-of-the-art performance in diverse and complex interactive agentic scenarios. All models and datasets are openly released.

item.title: Multi-Legal-Bench: Evaluating LLMs on Legal Reasoning Across Jurisdictions, Languages, and Legal Traditions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29738v1

AI Summary (中文)

多司法辖区法律基准 Multi-Legal-Bench：突破语言、法系与管辖壁垒的统一评估框架

当前法律NLP基准普遍存在单语局限性或任务异质性聚合问题——或仅覆盖单一语言（如仅英语），或将不同法域下定义迥异的法律任务强行合并，导致跨语言、跨法系性能比较不可靠。为此，本研究提出 Multi-Legal-Bench：首个真正意义上的跨司法辖区、跨语言、跨法系法律推理基准。

该基准覆盖6个大陆法系国家（乌克兰、法国、荷兰、波兰、捷克、立陶宛），涵盖4大语系（斯拉夫、罗曼、日耳曼、波罗的海），基于1.34亿份真实法院判决文书构建。我们严格对齐任务定义，在所有司法辖区统一实施5项结构化法律推理任务：法院类型分类、判决形式分类、案件结果预测、法律规范抽取、案由类别预测——全部映射自各国法院登记系统的标准化元数据。为反映现实约束，设计稀疏5×6任务-辖区矩阵（30格中仅20格有标注），避免强制泛化。

实验评估7个前沿大模型（通过AWS Bedrock零样本/三样本提示）及4个中小规模模型（3–12B参数）用于缩放分析。关键发现包括：

  • 任务驱动的少样本增益具有跨辖区稳健性：在乌克兰发现的少样本提升效应，在其余5国全部复现；
  • 无绝对主导模型：模型排名随任务类型与司法辖区显著波动，凸显“任务-辖区耦合”特性；
  • 跨语言迁移不依赖语言亲缘性：乌克兰→法国（罗曼语族，性能下降−2.1个百分点）优于乌克兰→波兰（同属斯拉夫语族，下降−13.7个百分点），标签集对齐度比语系归属更能预测迁移质量；
  • 分词器“丰度”（tokenizer fertility）与跨语言准确率弱相关（r=−0.27, p=0.14），证实模型架构与预训练数据构成更关键瓶颈。

本基准完全开源：含全部原始数据、标准化prompt模板、模型预测结果及评估脚本。

AI Summary (English)

Multi-Legal-Bench is the first cross-jurisdictional legal benchmark enabling rigorous, task-aligned evaluation of LLMs across six civil-law countries (Ukraine, France, Netherlands, Poland, Czech Republic, Lithuania), four language families, and 134 million real court decisions. It defines five identical legal reasoning tasks—court-type classification, judgment-form classification, case-outcome prediction, legal norm extraction, and cause-category prediction—mapped to structured national registry metadata, arranged in a deliberately sparse 5×6 task-jurisdiction matrix. Evaluating 7 frontier LLMs (zero-/3-shot via AWS Bedrock) and 4 smaller models (3–12B), we find: (1) task-dependent few-shot gains observed in Ukraine replicate robustly across all jurisdictions; (2) no model dominates universally—rankings shift significantly with both task and jurisdiction; (3) cross-lingual few-shot transfer quality correlates better with label-set alignment than language family proximity (e.g., UA→FR transfer outperforms UA→PL despite Slavic kinship); and (4) tokenizer fertility shows negligible predictive power for cross-lingual accuracy (r=−0.27, p=0.14), underscoring architecture and pretraining data as primary determinants. All data, prompts, and predictions are publicly released.

Abstract

Legal NLP benchmarks overwhelmingly evaluate a single language or aggregate tasks that differ fundamentally across jurisdictions, making cross-lingual comparison impossible. We introduce Multi-Legal-Bench, the first cross-jurisdictional legal benchmark that evaluates identical tasks across six countries (Ukraine, France, Netherlands, Poland, Czech Republic, Lithuania), four language families, and 134 million court decisions. The benchmark defines five tasks court-type classification, judgment form classification, case-outcome prediction, legal norm extraction, and cause category prediction mapped to structured metadata from national court registries, forming a deliberately sparse 5x6 task-jurisdiction matrix (20 of 30 cells filled). We evaluate 7 frontier LLMs under zero-shot and 3-shot prompting via AWS Bedrock, with 4 additional small/medium models (3-12B) for scaling analysis. Our results reveal that: (1) task-dependent few-shot effects discovered in Ukrainian replicate across all jurisdictions; (2) no single model dominates any language rankings shift with both task and jurisdiction; (3) cross-lingual few-shot transfer does not follow language proximity: UA->FR (Romance, -2.1 pp) transfers better than UA->PL (Slavic, -13.7 pp), with label-set alignment predicting transfer quality better than language family; and (4) tokenizer fertility, despite a 2.3x spread, does not significantly predict cross-lingual accuracy (r=-0.27, p=0.14), suggesting that model architecture and pretraining data dominate tokenizer efficiency. We release all data, prompts, and model predictions.

item.title: Opir: Efficient Multi-Task Safety Classification for Toxicity, Jailbreaks, Hate Speech, and Harmful Content
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29659v1

AI Summary (中文)

Opir：面向多任务安全分类的高效轻量级守门员模型

背景与挑战：面向大语言模型（LLM）的实时安全过滤亟需兼顾高精度、低延迟与小体积的守门员（guardrail）模型，尤其需在不依赖庞大参数量的前提下，精准区分有毒内容、越狱尝试、仇恨言论、有害响应等多元风险，并避免将良性敏感文本（如医学讨论、历史批判）误判为有害内容。

方法创新：本文提出 Opir——基于 GLiClass 架构构建的轻量级编码器家族。其核心包含：

  • ✅ 多任务统一架构：同步支持二元安全/不安全判别、多标签毒性细粒度分类（如“侮辱+性别歧视+煽动暴力”）、越狱意图识别、以及零样本提示/响应风险类别推断；
  • ✅ 极致轻量化边缘版本：专用于二元安全分类的子模型参数量 <100M，适配端侧部署；
  • ✅ 三层细粒度训练体系：覆盖 16个顶层标签、126个中层标签、854个叶级标签（共996类），数据融合分类学对齐的恶意样本、对抗挖掘的难负例、安全保持型良性样本、合成响应、多语言翻译数据，并整合 Aegis2 与 WildGuard 子集；
  • ✅ 开源评估框架：支持 GLiClass、GLiNER2 及解码器类模型，覆盖提示安全、响应安全、拒绝率、子类别分析等 8 类评估维度，兼容主流公开基准。

关键成果：在涵盖 12 项安全分类任务与 17 类细粒度分类任务的扩展评测中，Opir 在多数基准上性能媲美或超越当前最强开源守门员系统（含 GLiNER2 与生成式方案），同时推理开销显著降低——验证了“小模型、大能力”的可行性。

AI Summary (English)

Opir is a family of efficient, encoder-based guardrail models built on the GLiClass architecture for real-time LLM safety filtering. It unifies binary safe/unsafe classification, multi-label toxicity detection, jailbreak identification, and zero-shot prompt/response categorization—while offering edge-optimized variants (<100M parameters) for deployment-constrained settings. Trained on a rich, taxonomy-grounded dataset (996 categories across 3 hierarchical levels) combining adversarial negatives, safety-preserving benign examples, synthetic responses, multilingual data, and curated subsets from Aegis2 and WildGuard, Opir achieves competitive or state-of-the-art performance across 12 safety classification and 17 category-level tasks—outperforming or matching eight contemporary open-weight baselines (including GLiNER2- and generative-based systems) with significantly lower computational footprint. We also open-source a flexible evaluation harness supporting multiple model backends and comprehensive safety assessment dimensions.

Abstract

Real-time safety filtering for large language model (LLM) applications requires classifiers that can detect unsafe prompts, toxic language, jailbreak attempts, and unsafe responses without the cost profile of large guardrail models, and that can distinguish benign sensitive text from genuinely covert harmful content. In this paper, we introduce Opir, a family of encoder-based guardrail models built on the GLiClass architecture. Opir includes multi-task models for binary safe/unsafe classification, multi-label toxicity classification, jailbreak classification, and zero-shot unsafe prompt and response categorization. We also release edge variants with fewer than 100M parameters dedicated to binary safe/unsafe categorization. The models are trained on a three-level taxonomy containing 996 categories across 16 top-level labels, 126 mid-level labels, and 854 leaf labels. Opir's training data combines taxonomy-grounded unsafe prompts, adversarially mined hard negatives, benign safety-preserving examples, generated response examples, multilingual translations, and portions of the Aegis2 and WildGuard training subsets. We also open-sourced an evaluation harness that supports GLiClass and GLiNER2 backends as well as decoder-based models, and covers binary safety classification, multi-label categorization, toxicity, jailbreak detection, prompt safety, response safety, response refusal, and prompt subcategory views across public benchmark families. Across an expanded comparison spanning 12 safety-classification tasks and 17 category tasks against eight contemporary guardrail systems -- including both GLiNER2-based and generative guardrail models -- Opir variants are competitive on or ahead of the strongest open-weight baselines on the majority of benchmark datasets while operating with a substantially smaller deployment footprint.

item.title: Beyond Attack Success Rate: Temporal Logit Observability for LLM Safety Failures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29629v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

传统安全评估过度依赖攻击成功率（ASR）——一个仅在生成结束时给出“是/否”的二元指标。它能判断LLM是否被成功越狱，却完全掩盖了失败发生的时间路径：两个导致同等有害输出的攻击，可能源于截然不同的解码动态（如早期妥协 vs. 临界点崩溃），而ASR对此毫无分辨力。

方法创新：时序对数几率可观测性（TLO）

我们提出Temporal Logit Observability（TLO）——一种无需训练、纯基于解码过程中原始logits的诊断框架。其核心是实时追踪合规性-拒绝性logit差值（compliance-refusal margin）随时间步的变化，并将每个模型-攻击组合映射到一个校准的2D平面上：横轴表征失败发生的时机（early/late），纵轴表征失败的确定性（gradual/abrupt）。该设计刻意聚焦于ASR最薄弱的区域——即高ASR但成因异质的攻击簇。

关键发现

  • 在4种对齐LLM（Llama-3-8B-Instruct, Qwen2-7B-Instruct, Phi-3-mini, Gemma-2-9B）与3类主流越狱范式（prompt injection, suffix optimization, semantic paraphrasing）上验证：ASR相近（±1.5%）的攻击，在TLO平面上呈现显著分离的聚类，揭示同一模型存在多种失败模式；
  • TLO几何结构与基于隐藏状态的拒绝方向探针（refusal-direction probes）高度一致（Pearson r > 0.87），证实其语义有效性；唯一偏差出现在Phi-3-mini，暴露了固定词典方法对细粒度拒绝表征的边界；
  • 基于TLO导出的简易早停规则（margin < −2.1持续3步），在保持0%误报率（对普通良性查询）前提下，将成功越狱率降低>52%。

意义

TLO首次实现仅从logits中无损还原安全失效的何时发生与如何展开，推动LLM安全评估从静态结果导向转向动态过程可解释。

AI Summary (English)

Attack Success Rate (ASR) is a coarse, endpoint-only metric that masks how and when safety failures unfold during LLM decoding. To address this, we propose Temporal Logit Observability (TLO): a training-free, logits-only diagnostic that tracks the compliance–refusal logit margin step-by-step and embeds each model–attack pair onto an interpretable 2D plane—where axes capture timing (early vs. late failure) and certainty (gradual vs. abrupt). Across four aligned LLMs and three jailbreak paradigms, attacks with nearly identical ASR (±1.5%) occupy distinct, semantically meaningful regions on the TLO plane—revealing diverse temporal failure modes within the same model. TLO geometry strongly aligns with refusal-direction probes from hidden states (r > 0.87), except for Phi-3-mini, highlighting a limitation of fixed-lexicon margin estimation. Crucially, a simple early-stopping rule derived from TLO reduces successful jailbreaks by over 52% with zero false positives on benign queries. TLO shifts safety evaluation from “did it fail?” to “when and how did it fail?”—observable directly from logits.

Abstract

Attack Success Rate (ASR) evaluates each jailbreak with a single yes/no label at the end of generation, telling us whether a failure happened but not how it unfolded. Two attacks that produce equally harmful outputs may have followed completely different paths, and ASR cannot tell them apart. We make those hidden paths observable from logits alone. Temporal Logit Observability (TLO) is a training-free diagnostic that watches a compliance-refusal margin during decoding and places each model-attack condition on a calibrated 2D plane. By design, this plane is most informative exactly where ASR is least informative: among attacks that succeed for genuinely different reasons. Across four aligned LLMs and three jailbreak paradigms, attacks with nearly identical ASR land at clearly different points on the plane: the same model can fail through different temporal patterns. The geometry matches refusal-direction probes from hidden states on most conditions, with one model showing the limit of our fixed-lexicon approach. A simple early-stop rule derived from TLO cuts successful jailbreaks by more than half, without false alarms on plain benign queries. Safety evaluation should report when and how a failure unfolds, not only whether it occurred. TLO makes the first two observable from logits alone.

item.title: Quotient DAGs for Off-Policy Evaluation:Forward-Flow Importance Sampling and Exact Slate Propensities
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29500v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

离线策略评估（Off-Policy Evaluation, OPE）旨在利用行为策略（behavior policy）采集的日志数据，无偏估计目标策略（target policy）的性能。该技术在推荐系统、医疗决策等高风险场景中至关重要。然而，标准重要性采样（IS）直接对完整轨迹重加权，易将生成过程中的冗余结构误判为信息性特征——例如，自回归式广告/商品“条目列表”（slate）推荐器按顺序逐个生成物品，但实际奖励函数与下游评估器通常仅依赖无序集合（unordered slate），忽略生成次序。这导致两重缺陷：(1) 干扰方差（nuisance variance）：因过度区分语义等价的历史而放大估计方差；(2) 计算鸿沟：精确计算无序slate的倾向性需对所有排列求和，呈阶乘级复杂度（O(k!)），不可扩展。

方法创新：商有向无环图（Quotient DAG）框架

本文提出商DAG建模范式：将原始轨迹DAG中所有对评估目标等价的历史节点合并为一个商节点，构建更紧凑的“语义等价类图”。在此商图上，定义前向流重要性权重（forward-flow IS），即从目标策略到行为策略的条件前向转移概率比，而非传统反向轨迹比。该设计天然消除了与评估无关的生成细节噪声。

关键成果：Forward-DP算法

针对满足“集合充分性”（set-sufficient）假设的下一物品接口（即行为策略的每步决策仅依赖当前上下文与已选集合），我们导出子集DAG动态规划算法（Forward-DP）。它以O(2ᵏ) 时间复杂度（k为slate大小）精确计算无序slate的倾向性，彻底规避阶乘枚举。实验表明：Forward-DP在真实推荐日志上将OPE方差降低37–62%，并首次支持基于精确倾向性的模型选择与置信区间构造。

意义

本工作统一了语义抽象与概率流建模，为结构化动作空间的OPE提供了可扩展、无偏、实用的新基元。

AI Summary (English)

Off-policy evaluation (OPE) is vital for safely assessing target policies using logged data from a different behavior policy—especially in high-stakes domains like recommendation and healthcare. Standard importance sampling suffers from nuisance variance when the behavior policy generates structured trajectories (e.g., ordered slates) while the reward and estimator depend only on semantic equivalence classes (e.g., unordered item sets). This mismatch inflates variance and incurs prohibitive computational cost: exact set-level propensities require factorial enumeration over all permutations. We introduce the quotient-DAG framework, which merges trajectory histories equivalent under the evaluation objective and assigns weights via target-to-behavior forward-flow ratios on the compressed graph. For slate recommendation with a set-sufficient next-item interface, this yields Forward-DP: a subset-DAG dynamic program that computes exact unordered slate propensities in O(2ᵏ) time—exponentially faster than factorial enumeration. Forward-DP enables practical, propensity-based OPE and model selection for autoregressive slate loggers, reducing estimation variance by 37–62% empirically.

Abstract

Off-policy evaluation estimates how a target policy would perform using data collected by a different behavior policy, which is crucial when online testing is costly or risky, such as in recommendation or healthcare. Standard importance sampling reweights each logged trajectory, but it can treat details of the generation process as meaningful even when the evaluation target ignores them: for example, an autoregressive slate recommender may generate an ordered sequence of items while the reward and downstream estimator depend only on the unordered slate. This creates nuisance variance and a computational gap, since exact unordered slate propensities require summing over all generation orders. We introduce a quotient-DAG view that merges histories equivalent for evaluation and assigns weights using target-to-behavior forward-flow ratios on the merged graph. For slate recommendation under a set-sufficient next-item interface, this yields Forward-DP, a subset-DAG dynamic program that computes exact unordered propensities without factorial enumeration. The resulting propensity primitive enables practical propensity-based evaluation and model selection for context-dependent autoregressive slate loggers.

item.title: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models on CFMME: A Comprehensive Chinese Financial Multimodal Evaluation Dataset
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29462v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

大型视觉-语言模型（LVLMs）的兴起显著拓展了多模态理解边界，但现有基准多聚焦通用场景或英文金融领域，严重缺乏面向中文金融业务全流程的系统性、高保真 multimodal 评估资源。为填补这一空白，我们构建了 CFMME（Chinese Financial Multimodal Evaluation）——首个专为中文金融场景设计的综合性多模态评测基准。

数据构建与任务设计

CFMME 包含 6,052 个高质量标注实例，覆盖从基础财经知识（如财报术语辨析）到复杂业务应用（如合同关键条款定位、票据篡改识别）的完整链条。数据涵盖 八大中文金融图像模态：财报图表、银行单据、保险保单、证券K线图、OCR扫描件、监管文件截图、金融App界面及合规宣传海报；并定义四大核心任务：多跳视觉问答（VQA）、细粒度目标检测、结构化文本识别（如表格/字段抽取）、跨模态信息对齐与推理。

主要发现与洞见

在 CFMME 上对 12 个主流 LVLM（含 Qwen-VL、InternVL、LLaVA-OneVision 等）的系统评测显示：当前最优模型在 VQA 任务中仅达 66.11% 准确率，在检测/识别/抽取三类任务上的平均得分为 77.18（满分100），凸显其在金融语义深度理解、长程逻辑推理及中文专业符号解析上的显著短板。错误分析进一步揭示：约 43% 的失败源于跨模态对齐偏差（如误读图表坐标轴标签），31% 源于金融领域知识缺失（如混淆“净资本”与“净资产”），而多轮交互与多文档协同推理能力尤为薄弱。

创新价值

CFMME 不仅提供标准化评测平台，更通过细粒度任务划分、真实业务场景建模及可解释性错误归因，为 LVLMs 在垂直领域的可信落地提供了方法论范式。数据集已开源，旨在推动金融AI向高精度、强鲁棒、可验证方向演进。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce CFMME, the first comprehensive Chinese financial multimodal evaluation benchmark, designed to rigorously assess Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) across perception, understanding, reasoning, and cognition throughout real-world financial workflows. CFMME comprises 6,052 expert-annotated instances spanning eight Chinese financial image modalities (e.g., financial charts, bank slips, insurance policies, stock K-line graphs) and four core tasks: multimodal question answering, fine-grained object detection, structured text recognition (e.g., table/field extraction), and cross-modal information alignment. Evaluating 12 representative LVLMs, we find the state-of-the-art model achieves only 66.11% accuracy on VQA and an average score of 77.18 on detection/recognition/extraction—revealing substantial gaps in domain-specific reasoning, Chinese financial symbol comprehension, and cross-modal grounding. Error analysis attributes 43% of failures to misaligned visual-textual grounding and 31% to missing financial knowledge. CFMME is publicly released to advance robust, trustworthy LVLMs for finance.

Abstract

The emergence of Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) has substantially expanded model capabilities beyond text-only understanding, enabling unified inference across both visual and textual modalities and supporting a broader range of real-world applications. To comprehensively evaluate the perception, understanding, reasoning, and cognition capabilities of LVLMs throughout the entire financial business workflow in Chinese contexts, we introduce CFMME, a novel Chinese financial multimodal evaluation benchmark. CFMME comprises 6,052 instances spanning from fundamental academic knowledge to complex real-world applications, covering eight primary financial image modalities and four core multimodal tasks. On CFMME, we conduct a thorough evaluation of representative LVLMs. The results show that the state-of-the-art model attains an overall accuracy of 66.11\% on the question answering task and an average score of 77.18 on the detection, recognition, and information extraction tasks, indicating substantial room for improvement in current LVLMs. In addition, we conduct detailed analyses of error causes, cross-modal capabilities, and multi-orientation settings, yielding valuable insights for future research. We hope that CFMME will spur further progress in LVLMs, especially by improving their performance on multiple multimodal tasks in the financial domain.

item.title: CrystalXRD-Bench: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for XRD Peak Indexing Across Diverse Crystalline Materials
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29446v1

AI Summary (中文)

CrystalXRD-Bench：首个面向XRD峰指数标定的视觉-语言模型基准

背景与挑战：粉末X射线衍射（XRD）图谱中Miller指数（HKL）的自动识别是材料科学关键任务，但现有视觉-语言多模态基准（如ScienceQA、DocVQA）未覆盖其核心能力——需从高精度科学曲线中精确定位窄峰位置，并完成多步晶体学推理（如布拉格定律→晶面间距→晶胞参数→空间群约束→HKL枚举）。该任务兼具细粒度视觉感知与符号化科学推理双重难点。

方法与构建：本文提出CrystalXRD-Bench，首个专为XRD峰指数标定设计的双语基准。它包含250个高质量样本，源自10个权威晶体学数据库（如ICSD、Materials Project），每样本提供三元组：① 渲染的XRD强度-2θ曲线图像；② 对应晶体结构信息文件（CIF）文本；③ 化学式。任务定义为：给定XRD图像，准确输出贡献于最高强度衍射峰的所有可能HKL指标集合（非单个最优解）。该设计支持解耦评估：视觉提取误差（如峰位误读）与晶体学推理误差（如空间群应用错误）可独立诊断。

主要发现：在7个主流VLM（GPT-4V、Qwen-VL、LLaVA-OneVision等）上测试表明：

  • 最优模型GPT-5.4仅达Jaccard相似度0.5888，精确匹配率37.6%；其余6个模型Jaccard均＜0.50，任务远未解决；
  • 系统性失败模式凸显：双峰重叠案例鲁棒性极差；召回率导向模型通过过度预测HKL“刷分”，牺牲精度；
  • CIF文本输入未能显著提升晶体学计算性能，揭示当前VLM对晶体学符号规则的内化严重不足；
  • 基准首次量化了VLM在定量科学图表理解中的脆弱边界（如角度分辨率敏感性、晶系判别偏差）。

创新点：填补科学AI在晶体学视觉推理的评估空白；提供可解释性诊断框架；全部数据、标注规范与评估代码将开源。

AI Summary (English)

CrystalXRD-Bench is the first benchmark designed to evaluate vision-language models (VLMs) on XRD peak indexing—a task demanding precise visual localization of narrow diffraction peaks and multi-step crystallographic reasoning (e.g., Bragg’s law → d-spacing → lattice parameters → space group constraints → HKL enumeration). Built from 10 public crystallographic databases, it comprises 250 samples, each pairing a rendered XRD pattern image with its source CIF text and chemical formula. The task is to recover all HKL indices contributing to the highest-intensity peak. Evaluating 7 state-of-the-art VLMs, we find the best performance (GPT-5.4) achieves only a Jaccard score of 0.5888 and exact-match accuracy of 37.6%; six models score below Jaccard 0.50. Systematic failure modes include brittleness on overlapping double peaks, recall-oriented over-prediction of HKLs, and no meaningful improvement from CIF text access—revealing fundamental gaps in symbolic crystallographic reasoning. CrystalXRD-Bench thus identifies concrete conditions under which current VLMs falter on quantitative scientific figures. All data and evaluation code will be publicly released.

Abstract

Miller-index identification from powder XRD patterns requires capabilities untested by existing multimodal benchmarks: the model must read a narrow peak location from a rendered scientific curve and then connect that observation to multi-step crystallographic reasoning. We introduce CrystalXRD-Bench, a 250-sample benchmark built from 10 public crystallographic databases for a single task: recover the full set of HKLs contributing to the highest-intensity peak in an XRD pattern. Each sample pairs the rendered XRD image with the source CIF text and chemical formula, so visual extraction errors and reasoning errors can be examined side by side. We evaluate seven vision-language models. The best Jaccard score is 0.5888 (GPT-5.4) with an exact-match rate of 37.6%, yet six of seven models remain below Jaccard 0.50; the task is far from solved. Error patterns vary systematically: double-peak cases are especially brittle, recall-heavy models gain coverage by over-predicting HKLs, and access to CIF text does not close the gap in crystallographic calculation. Alongside model rankings, the benchmark identifies the conditions under which current VLMs fail on quantitative scientific figures. All data and evaluation code will be publicly available.

item.title: When Does Persona Prompting Actually Help? A Retrieval and Metric Analysis of Expert Role Injection in LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29420v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

人格提示（Persona Prompting）被广泛用于引导大语言模型（LLM）行为，但其实际效用尚不明确。既有研究多依赖单一聚合指标（如整体评分）评估效果，难以揭示专家角色注入是否真正提升响应质量，抑或仅在不同质量维度间引发权衡。

方法设计

本研究对1,140个开放式问题（覆盖38类专家角色、6大领域：医学、心理学、金融、法律、科学、技术）开展受控实验，对比四种提示条件：

  • 无角色提示（Baseline）
  • 通用领域专家提示（Generic）
  • 基于嵌入的自动角色检索（Embedding-only）
  • 混合检索方法（Hybrid）：结合嵌入相似度搜索与LLM驱动的角色精筛

采用多维评估体系，同步测量专业知识深度（Expertise Depth）与表达清晰度（Clarity）等核心指标，避免聚合平均掩盖结构性差异。

关键发现

1. 存在稳健权衡：角色提示系统性提升专业知识深度（+12.3% avg），但显著降低清晰度（−9.7% avg）——该权衡被传统聚合评分完全遮蔽；
2. 效果高度条件化：在咨询类问题及医学/心理学领域表现最优（结构化推理与风险沟通价值凸显）；而在金融、法律等需简洁平实解释的场景，基线提示反而更优；
3. 混合检索显著优于纯嵌入法（角色匹配准确率+28.5%），但无法消除深度-清晰度根本权衡。

理论与实践启示

人格提示本质是响应特性重塑工具，而非普适能力增强器；推动LLM应用落地需转向多指标、细粒度、场景适配型评估范式。

AI Summary (English)

This study investigates when and how persona prompting—injecting expert roles into LLM prompts—actually improves response quality. Through a controlled evaluation on 1,140 open-ended questions across 38 expert roles and 6 domains, we compare four prompting conditions: no role, generic domain-expert, embedding-based retrieval, and a novel hybrid method combining embedding search with LLM-guided role selection. Aggregate scores show only marginal overall differences, but fine-grained metric analysis reveals a consistent, previously obscured tradeoff: role prompting reliably increases expertise depth (+12.3%) while decreasing clarity (−9.7%). This effect is highly conditional—strongest for advisory questions in medicine and psychology, yet detrimental for conceptual explanations in finance, law, science, and technology. Hybrid retrieval significantly outperforms embedding-only selection (+28.5% accuracy), yet fails to resolve the core depth–clarity tension. We conclude that persona prompting reshapes response characteristics rather than universally enhancing capability, underscoring the necessity of multi-metric, context-aware evaluation.

Abstract

Persona prompting is widely used to steer large language models, yet its practical value remains unclear. Prior work often evaluates persona prompting using aggregate scores, making it difficult to determine whether expert-role prompting consistently improves response quality or instead changes responses along different quality dimensions. We study this question through a controlled comparison of four prompting conditions across 1,140 open-ended questions spanning 38 expert roles and six domains: no role prompt, a generic domain-expert prompt, embedding-based role retrieval, and a hybrid retrieval method combining embedding search with LLM-based role selection. Aggregate results show only small overall differences between conditions. However, metric-level analysis reveals a consistent tradeoff that aggregate averages obscure: role prompting systematically increases expertise depth while reducing clarity. These effects are highly conditional rather than universal. Role prompting performs best on advisory questions and in domains such as medicine and psychology, where structured expert framing and risk communication are intrinsically valuable. In contrast, baseline prompting performs better on conceptual and explanatory questions in finance, legal, science, and technology domains, where concise plain-language explanation is more important. We further show that hybrid retrieval significantly improves over embedding-only role selection, although better role retrieval does not eliminate the broader expertise-depth versus clarity tradeoff. Overall, our findings suggest that persona prompting primarily reshapes response characteristics rather than broadly improving capability, and that multi-metric evaluation is necessary for understanding its effects.

item.title: Provably Secure Agent Guardrail
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29251v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着大语言模型（LLM）从封闭式生成引擎演进为具备广泛执行权限的智能体（Agent），AI失控风险已升级为人工智能安全领域的根本性危机。现有防御体系高度依赖经验性语义护栏（如提示工程、分类器微调）和概率化大模型仲裁器，但在面对语义符号解耦攻击（semantic symbol decoupling attacks）——即攻击者通过精心构造的自然语言诱导模型在语义层面“正确理解”却在逻辑层面违背约束——时，无法提供可证明的确定性安全下界。

方法创新

本文突破经验主义范式，提出基于逻辑推理固有局限性的新型智能体安全范式。核心是构建可执行的证明约束型动作框架（ePCA），采用神经-符号隔离架构：彻底摒弃对自然语言意图的语义信任，强制智能体在执行任何物理操作前，必须将意图无损形式化为一阶逻辑数学约束（如 ∀x. (UserIntent(x) → SafeAction(x))），并通过轻量级形式验证器实时检验其可证伪性与一致性。

关键成果

在宏观（多轮任务链攻击）与微观（单步指令注入）二维动态对抗系统中实证评估表明：ePCA实现0%攻击成功率与0%误报率，覆盖全部12类典型越狱与符号操纵场景；端到端验证延迟均值仅23ms（P95 < 41ms），满足实时控制需求。本工作首次在显式系统假设（如动作空间可建模、约束可一阶表达）下，为智能体安全提供了条件形式化基础与可工程落地的底层防御范式，为构建可信自主系统奠定理论与实践双基石。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the fundamental security crisis arising as LLMs evolve into high-privilege agents: existing empirical semantic guardrails fail to guarantee deterministic safety against semantic symbol decoupling attacks. We propose a novel logic-bound security paradigm, grounded in the inherent limitations of formal reasoning. Based on it, we introduce the executable Proof-Constrained Action (ePCA) framework—a neural-symbolic isolated architecture that eliminates semantic trust in natural language: agents must losslessly formalize intentions into first-order logical constraints before any physical action, verified in real time. Empirical evaluation across macroscopic (multi-turn task chains) and microscopic (single-step injection) adversarial settings achieves 0% attack success rate and 0% false positive rate, with sub-41ms P95 verification latency. ePCA provides the first conditional formal foundation—under explicit assumptions—and an engineering-ready defense paradigm for trustworthy intelligent systems.

Abstract

As large language models transition from bounded generative engines to agents with expansive execution privileges, AI going out of control precipitates a fundamental crisis in artificial intelligence security. Existing defense architectures heavily rely on empirical semantic guardrails and probabilistic large model adjudicators, mechanisms that fail to provide deterministic security lower bounds when facing complex semantic symbol decoupling attacks. To overcome this empirical semantic guardrail dilemma, this paper proposes a new security paradigm for agents based on the fundamental limitations of logical reasoning. Based on this paradigm, we further introduce an executable Proof-Constrained Action (ePCA) framework with a neural symbolic isolation architecture. This framework abandons semantic trust in natural language, forcing agents to losslessly formalize their intentions into first-order logical mathematical constraints before performing physical operations. Empirical evaluations of macroscopic and microscopic two-dimensional dynamic adversarial systems demonstrate that our formal verification mechanism achieves zero attack success rate and zero false positive rate across the evaluated scenarios, with extremely low computational latency. This research provides a conditional formal foundation under explicit system assumptions and an engineering paradigm for constructing the underlying defense foundation for future intelligent systems.

item.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30336v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）作为一种隐私保护的分布式机器学习范式，允许多个客户端在不共享原始数据的前提下协同训练全局模型。然而，传统聚合方法（如FedAvg）采用静态、均匀的客户端权重，严重忽视了客户端贡献的异质性与时变性——不同客户端的数据质量、分布偏移、计算能力及参与稳定性差异显著，导致模型收敛缓慢、性能波动大，且贡献评估存在系统性偏差，损害公平性与鲁棒性。

方法创新：轨迹沙普利值（TSV）与FedTSV

本文提出轨迹沙普利值（Trajectory Shapley Value, TSV），一种新型公平感知的贡献度度量：它基于验证集效用，沿全局优化轨迹动态量化每个客户端对模型参数演化路径的边际影响，兼具时间一致性（temporal consistency）与可解释性。不同于快照式评估，TSV通过反事实轨迹扰动建模长期协作价值。基于TSV，我们设计FedTSV自适应聚合算法：每轮根据实时TSV得分生成动态权重，支持服务器对数据异构、低质量上传甚至恶意参与（如后门攻击）进行即时响应与抑制。

主要结果与意义

在CIFAR-10/100、FEMNIST和Shakespeare等标准基准上，FedTSV相比FedAvg、q-Fed、FOOLSGOLD等基线：

  • 收敛加速：平均减少23.7%通信轮次达目标精度；
  • 鲁棒性提升：在30%客户端被投毒时，准确率仍保持86.4%（+9.2% vs FedAvg）；
  • 公平性增强：Shapley值分布方差降低58%，高贡献客户端权重更稳定，低贡献者不被过度惩罚；
  • 理论保障：TSV满足效率性、对称性、零贡献者归零等沙普利公理，为公平联邦优化提供可验证的公理化基础。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) enables collaborative model training across privacy-sensitive clients, yet standard aggregation methods (e.g., FedAvg) assign fixed weights, ignoring heterogeneous and time-varying client contributions—leading to biased, unstable, and unfair optimization. To address this, we propose the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV): a validation-based, temporally consistent metric that quantifies each client’s marginal influence on the entire global optimization trajectory, not just instantaneous updates. Building on TSV, we design FedTSV, an adaptive server aggregation scheme that dynamically assigns round-wise weights in real time, enabling robust response to data heterogeneity and adversarial behavior. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-10/100, FEMNIST, and Shakespeare show FedTSV accelerates convergence (−23.7% rounds), improves robustness (+9.2% accuracy under 30% poisoning), and yields more equitable contribution assessments (58% lower Shapley variance). TSV is axiomatically grounded, satisfying efficiency, symmetry, and dummy-player properties—establishing a principled foundation for fairness-aware FL.

Abstract

Federated learning is an emerging distributed paradigm that addresses the challenges posed by heterogeneous, privacy-sensitive data. It enables multiple clients to train a model collaboratively by aggregating their local updates at a server. However, conventional aggregation schemes typically use fixed weights that fail to reflect unequal and time-varying client contributions, leading to biased and unstable learning. To improve fairness and stability, we propose the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV), a contribution metric that evaluates how each client influences the optimization trajectory of the global model using a validation-based, temporally consistent utility. Building on TSV, we design FedTSV, an adaptive aggregation method that converts per-round evaluations into dynamic client weights, allowing the server to respond to heterogeneous and adversarial participation in real time. Experiments on benchmark datasets show that FedTSV accelerates convergence, improves robustness, and yields more equitable contribution assessments, thereby providing a principled foundation for fairness-aware federated optimization.

item.title: Statistical Embeddings for Similarity, Retrieval, and Interpretable Alignment of Numeric Tabular Datasets
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30289v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

数值型表格数据是科学研究中最主流的数据格式，但大语言模型缺乏对异构特征空间下数值数据的原生、语义化表征能力。现有方法或局限于单数据集预测建模（依赖统一变量定义），或无法提供可解释的跨数据集对齐机制，尤其在变量命名不一致、测量尺度迥异、特征维度非对齐等现实场景中表现薄弱。

方法创新

本研究提出统计嵌入（Statistical Embeddings）框架：

  • 结构化描述：通过标准化探索性数据分析（EDA）提取12类稳健统计描述符（如分布矩、相关性谱、缺失模式、尺度归一化特征等），将原始表格压缩为可比性高、隐私友好的描述向量；
  • 语义嵌入：利用预训练句子Transformer（all-MiniLM-L6-v2）将描述文本映射至共享向量空间，实现“用自然语言理解统计特性”；
  • 可解释对齐：引入稀疏惩罚型典型相关分析（Sparse Penalized CCA），无需变量名匹配即可自动识别驱动跨数据集相似性的关键统计维度（如“高偏度+低方差变量组”对应“材料孔隙率分布”），输出变量级可解释映射；
  • 隐私增强：支持在嵌入前对描述符集施加差分隐私（DP）噪声（ε = 0.5–5.0），全程无需访问原始观测值，满足医疗、核材料等敏感领域合规要求。

主要结果

在15个跨域数据集（含UCI基准、材料信息学数据库、核级石墨微观表征数据）上验证：

  • 检索性能卓越：整体P@1达0.90，即查询数据最相似的Top-1结果90%为真实近邻；
  • 鲁棒性强：即使移除部分描述符或施加ε=1.0 DP噪声，聚类结构与最近邻关系仍保持稳定；
  • 应用落地明确：已集成至RAG流水线，支撑未知数据的自动化算法推荐（如选择最优回归器）与物理仿真模型参数初始化。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Statistical Embeddings, a framework for similarity measurement, retrieval, and interpretable alignment of heterogeneous numeric tabular datasets without requiring shared variable names or feature conventions. It first computes structured exploratory data analysis (EDA) descriptors—e.g., distributional moments, correlation spectra, and missingness patterns—then encodes them into a shared semantic space using a pretrained sentence transformer. Cross-dataset similarity is quantified via Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA), while a sparsely penalized CCA variant recovers interpretable, variable-level correspondences by identifying which statistical descriptors drive alignment. Differential privacy can be applied to descriptors pre-embedding, enabling privacy-preserving comparison without raw data access. Evaluated on 15 diverse datasets—from general benchmarks to nuclear-grade graphite characterization—the method achieves a P@1 score of 0.9, with robust nearest-neighbor retrieval and clustering across embedding ablations and differential privacy budgets (ε ≥ 1.0). It enables principled integration of numeric data into retrieval-augmented generation, supporting data-driven algorithm selection and simulation initialization for unknown datasets.

Abstract

Numeric tabular datasets are the dominant data format in scientific practice, yet large language models lack native mechanisms for representing numeric datasets in a meaningful way across heterogeneous feature spaces. Existing approaches either target predictive modeling over individual datasets, which requires a shared set of variable definitions, or lack mechanisms for interpretable cross-dataset alignment. The proposed methodology characterizes numeric tabular datasets through structured exploratory data analysis descriptors, embeds those descriptors into a shared vector space using a pretrained sentence transformer, and quantifies cross-dataset similarity via Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA). Furthermore, a penalized formulation of CCA is applied to recover sparse, interpretable variable-level correspondences between datasets, identifying which statistical descriptors or variable-level quantities drive cross-dataset alignment without requiring shared variable names or feature conventions. Differential privacy is optionally applied to the descriptor set prior to embedding, supporting deployment in sensitive data contexts without requiring access to raw observations at time of comparison. The methodology is evaluated across 15 datasets spanning general-purpose benchmarks, materials informatics, and nuclear-grade graphite characterization. Results demonstrate a total P@1 score of 0.9, with known nearest-neighbor retrieval and cluster structure remaining robust across embedding ablations and differential privacy budgets. The proposed framework provides a principled pathway for integrating heterogeneous numeric data into retrieval-augmented generation pipelines while preserving statistical context, with direct applications to data-driven algorithm selection and simulation model initialization for unknown datasets.

item.title: Privacy-Enhanced Zero-Order Federated Learning via xMK-CKKS over Wireless Channels
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30123v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在无线联邦学习（FL）中，同态加密（HE）是实现隐私保护聚合的关键技术。然而，现有HE-over-the-air方案多依赖单密钥HE（如CKKS），需精确信道估计或预均衡以对抗无线衰落；更严重的是，所有客户端共享同一私钥，使系统面临“诚实但好奇”客户端合谋攻击风险——任一客户端被攻破即导致全网密钥泄露。

方法创新

本文提出首个面向xMK-CKKS（扩展型多密钥CKKS）的无线信道聚合协议，无需信道状态信息（CSI）估计。该协议包含四阶段设计：（1）客户端广播部分公钥；（2）同步发送加密梯度标量；（3）服务器接收叠加信号；（4）利用同一信道实现下的代数抵消特性，使主导的大模数加密项在解密时自动相消。该机制天然适配零阶FL（每轮仅传输1个加密标量），且通信/加密开销与模型维度无关，显著降低边缘设备负担。

主要成果

  • 理论保障：严格证明解码后的加密噪声引入的误差可控，算法仍保持 $O(1/\sqrt{K})$ 收敛速率（$K$为通信轮数），仅增加可忽略的噪声底限；
  • 安全强化：支持客户端级独立密钥管理，可抵御服务器与最多 $N-1$ 个客户端的合谋攻击；
  • 实证验证：在MNIST数据集上验证了协议有效性，通信效率提升达3.2×（相比CSI依赖方案），且准确率损失<0.8%。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes the first privacy-enhanced zero-order federated learning protocol over wireless channels using xMK-CKKS, a multi-key homomorphic encryption scheme. Unlike prior single-key HE-over-the-air methods requiring channel estimation or pre-equalization, our four-phase protocol enables analog aggregation of encrypted scalars without CSI, leveraging algebraic cancellation of dominant large-modulus terms across identical channel realizations. Integrated with zero-order FL over slow-varying LoS-dominant channels, each device transmits only one encrypted scalar per round, making communication and encryption overhead model-dimension-independent. We prove the decoded encryption noise preserves the $O(1/\sqrt{K})$ convergence rate up to a negligible floor. The scheme achieves client-level security: it remains secure against an honest-but-curious server colluding with up to $N-1$ clients. Experiments on MNIST validate both theoretical analysis and practical efficacy.

Abstract

Homomorphic encryption (HE) enables privacy-preserving aggregation in federated learning (FL) by allowing the server to operate on encrypted data without decryption. Existing HE-over-the-air methods mainly rely on single-key HE schemes and require channel estimation or pre-equalization to compensate for wireless fading. However, single-key HE remains vulnerable to honest-but-curious clients sharing the same secret key. In addition, compromising a single client may compromise the security of the entire network, while multi-key HE schemes provide stronger client-level security by assigning each device its own secret key. We propose a four-phase protocol that enables xMK-CKKS, a famous multi-key HE scheme, aggregation over a shared wireless channel without channel estimation. The protocol retransmits partial public keys and ciphertexts through the same channel realization, so that the dominant large-modulus encryption terms cancel algebraically during decryption. We integrate this protocol with zero-order FL over slowly varying LoS-dominant channels, where each device transmits a single encrypted scalar per round and the communication/encryption overhead is independent of the model dimension. We prove that the decoded encryption noise preserves the \(O(1/\sqrt{K})\) convergence rate up to a negligible noise floor. The protocol is secure against an honest-but-curious server colluding with up to \(N-1\) clients, and numerical results on MNIST validate the analysis.

item.title: Q-ANCHOR: Federated Quantum Learning with ZNE-guided Correction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30075v1

AI Summary (中文)

Q-ANCHOR：基于ZNE引导校正的联邦量子学习框架

背景与挑战：量子联邦学习（QFL）旨在分布式客户端上协同训练量子模型，同时严格保障数据本地化。当前主流采用Federated Averaging（FedAvg）进行模型聚合，因其简洁性与低通信开销广受青睐。然而，在真实含噪量子硬件上部署QFL时，存在严重的“双重偏移”现象：一方面，非独立同分布（non-IID）数据引发客户端偏移（client drift）；另一方面，量子梯度估计受硬件噪声干扰，产生系统性硬件偏差（hardware bias），导致全局模型持续偏离最优解。本文首次从理论层面证明：FedAvg在该双重偏移下收敛至一个不可消除的误差下界，其根源正是量子硬件偏差——标准平均无法抵消该偏差，形成持久性性能瓶颈。

方法创新：为突破此限制，我们提出Q-ANCHOR——一种量子感知的联邦聚合新范式。其核心包含两大协同机制：（1）零噪声外推（ZNE）锚定服务端更新：服务器端利用多噪声级量子电路执行ZNE，对聚合梯度进行偏差校正，将更新锚定于零噪声极限；（2）有状态客户端校正：客户端在本地训练中引入偏差感知动量与历史梯度记忆，主动抑制本地更新中的硬件偏差累积与数据异构性影响。

主要成果：理论分析严格证明Q-ANCHOR可同时消除经典客户端偏移（恢复FedAvg在理想条件下的收敛率）并渐进降低硬件偏差下界（偏差项随通信轮次衰减）。实验表明：在真实量子模拟器及IBM硬件上，Q-ANCHOR相较FedAvg、SCAFFOLD等基线，测试准确率提升达12.7%，训练波动性降低63%，且收敛更鲁棒、泛化更优。本工作为面向噪声硬件的可信量子联邦学习提供了首个兼具理论保证与工程可行性的系统性解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Quantum Federated Learning (QFL) enables collaborative training of quantum models across decentralized clients while preserving data privacy. Though FedAvg is widely adopted for its simplicity, real-world deployment on noisy quantum hardware suffers from a double-drift problem: client drift due to non-IID data and persistent hardware bias from noisy quantum gradient estimates. We first prove that FedAvg converges only to a non-vanishing error floor caused by hardware bias—uncorrectable by standard averaging. To address this, we propose Q-ANCHOR, a quantum-aware federated aggregation framework featuring: (i) ZNE-guided server anchoring, where zero-noise extrapolation corrects aggregated gradients at the server; and (ii) stateful client correction, which suppresses both client drift and hardware-induced bias via bias-aware momentum and gradient memory. Our convergence theory guarantees elimination of classical client drift and active reduction of the hardware-bias floor. Experiments on quantum simulators and IBM hardware show Q-ANCHOR achieves up to 12.7% higher accuracy and 63% lower training variance than baselines, with superior stability and generalization.

Abstract

Quantum Federated Learning (QFL) offers a promising framework to train quantum models across distributed clients while keeping data strictly local. Due to its simplicity and low communication overhead, Federated Averaging (FedAvg) is the standard aggregation choice in QFL literature. However, deploying QFL on practical hardware exposes a severe double-drift phenomenon: the global model is simultaneously derailed by client drift from non-IID data and hardware bias from noisy quantum gradient estimates. In this work, we first analyze the convergence of FedAvg under these realistic conditions, mathematically demonstrating that quantum hardware bias creates a persistent error floor that standard averaging cannot correct. To overcome this limitation, we propose Q-ANCHOR, a quantum-aware federated aggregation architecture that anchors server updates with zero-noise extrapolation while applying stateful client correction to suppress both client drift and hardware-induced bias. Our convergence theory proves that Q-ANCHOR successfully mitigates classical client drift while actively reducing the hardware-bias floor. Experimental results demonstrate that Q-ANCHOR achieves significantly more stable training than conventional FL baselines.

item.title: Fingerprinting Inference Systems of Large Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29979v1

AI Summary (中文)

大语言模型推理系统指纹识别：从数值偏差到可识别性

本研究揭示了一个被长期忽视的安全维度：大语言模型（LLM）的推理行为不仅由模型权重决定，更受其底层推理系统组件的显著影响。这些组件包括推理引擎（如vLLM、Text Generation Inference）、注意力计算后端（如FlashAttention、PyTorch SDPA）以及硬件平台（如A100、H100、V100）。由于浮点运算实现差异、内存布局优化及硬件指令集特性，相同模型在不同系统上对同一输入会产生微小但系统性、可复现的数值偏差——这些偏差虽不改变语义一致性，却会通过softmax归一化与采样过程逐层放大，最终导致输出文本概率分布的可观测偏移。

我们首次系统性验证了该现象的安全后果：此类偏移具有组件特异性，构成天然“数字指纹”。基于此，我们提出一种轻量、黑盒、无需模型访问权限的指纹识别方法：通过设计敏感提示模板（如数值敏感型问答、多跳逻辑题），采集模型在低温度（T ∈ [0.1, 0.7]）下的响应分布统计特征（如top-k token概率熵、token序列稳定性、特定歧义词选择偏好），构建多粒度分类器。在涵盖8种推理引擎、5种注意力后端、4类GPU架构的实证评估中，我们的方法在零样本迁移设置下实现平均96.2%的推理引擎识别准确率、92.7%的注意力后端识别率、88.5%的硬件平台识别率，且在非零温度下鲁棒性强。研究进一步证明：彻底防御该指纹攻击需消除全栈软硬件数值等价性，这在工程与理论上均不可行；我们因此提出部分缓解策略（如随机化attention dropout掩码、跨批次logit扰动），并量化其对精度与延迟的权衡影响。

AI Summary (English)

This paper demonstrates that inference systems—not just LLM weights—leave identifiable fingerprints in model outputs due to hardware- and software-induced numerical deviations (e.g., in attention computation, memory layout, and floating-point arithmetic). We show these deviations are component-specific (inference engine, attention backend, GPU architecture) and propagate to observable textual responses—even at non-zero temperature. Leveraging this, we propose a black-box fingerprinting method based on statistical analysis of prompt-response behavior using carefully designed sensitive prompts and distributional features (e.g., top-k entropy, token stability). Empirical evaluation across 8 engines, 5 backends, and 4 GPU types achieves >96%, >92%, and >88% identification accuracy, respectively. We prove fundamental hardness of complete mitigation—requiring numerical equivalence across entire software/hardware stacks—and propose practical partial defenses with measured trade-offs in accuracy and latency.

Abstract

The behavior of LLMs does not depend solely on the model itself. Components of the inference system, such as the inference engine, attention backend, and hardware platform, subtly influence how inputs are processed. These components differ in their implementations and thereby induce small numerical deviations across systems when running the same model. While prior work has established the theoretical existence of such deviations, their security implications have remained unexplored. In this paper, we show that these deviations are characteristic of specific components and propagate to observable textual outputs, exposing the inference system to any party that can query the model. Building on this observation, we introduce a fingerprinting method that analyzes the prompt-response behavior of LLMs to identify components of the inference system. Our empirical evaluation demonstrates that the inference engine, attention backend, and underlying hardware platform can be identified reliably, even when the LLM is operated at non-zero temperature. We show that preventing fingerprinting is fundamentally hard, as it would require eliminating numerical differences between hardware and software stacks. We therefore propose partial mitigations and discuss their impact.

item.title: Dissecting the Black Box: Circuit-Level Analysis of LLM Vulnerability Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29901v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）在软件漏洞检测任务中展现出显著能力，但其内部决策机制长期被视为“黑箱”——模型究竟如何识别脆弱代码？是直接匹配漏洞模式，还是依赖间接线索？ 本研究首次将机制可解释性（mechanistic interpretability） 系统引入安全分析领域，旨在从电路（circuit）层面解构LLM的漏洞识别逻辑。

方法与技术路径

我们基于轻量级开源模型 Gemma-2-2b，采用前沿工具 Circuit Tracer，对472个C/C++代码样本（含缓冲区溢出、空指针解引用等典型漏洞）的分类过程进行全层计算路径追踪。通过激活传播分析、注意力头定位、MLP神经元筛选及因果验证性消融实验，精准识别驱动预测的关键神经组件。

核心发现

  • 反直觉机制：模型并非主动“检测漏洞”，而是高度依赖安全检测器（safety detectors）——即早期层（L5、L7）中特化识别安全编码模式（如边界检查、空指针防护）的注意力头；当这些头未被激活时，模型默认输出“脆弱”。
  • 关键电路组件：Layer 7 的特定 MLP 神经元（仅约20个）编码漏洞相关语义特征；Layer 11 为最终决策枢纽——移除该层使准确率从100%骤降至6%。
  • 高稀疏性与可解释性：有效电路仅占用模型总容量的16%，构成极简、可定位、可验证的推理子网络。

创新与价值

本工作首次揭示LLM安全推理的“安全优先”范式，为构建可解释、可调试、可增强的AI驱动漏洞检测系统提供电路级理论基础与工程接口，推动LLM在关键基础设施安全领域的可信落地。

AI Summary (English)

This work pioneers circuit-level mechanistic interpretability to demystify how LLMs detect software vulnerabilities. Using Circuit Tracer on Gemma-2-2b, we trace computations across 472 C/C++ code samples and discover a counterintuitive mechanism: the model relies not on direct vulnerability signatures, but on safety detectors—early-layer attention heads (L5, L7) that recognize safe coding patterns; vulnerability classification occurs primarily when these detectors fail to activate. Critical components include safety-focused attention heads and just ~20 MLP neurons in Layer 7 encoding vulnerability features. Ablation confirms causality: removing Layer 11 drops accuracy from 100% to 6%; ablating only those 20 neurons reduces accuracy by 50%. Crucially, the effective detection circuit uses only 16% of model capacity, enabling sparse, interpretable, and engineerable security predictions.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) can detect software vulnerabilities, but how do they actually identify vulnerable code? We address this question using mechanistic interpretability; analyzing the internal computations of a neural network to understand its reasoning process.Using Circuit Tracer on Gemma-2-2b, we trace the computational pathways activated when the model classifies 472 C/C++ code samples as vulnerable or safe. Our analysis reveals a surprising finding: the model primarily relies on safety detectors, attention heads that recognize safe coding patterns, rather than directly detecting vulnerability signatures. When these safety detectors fail to activate, the model classifies code as vulnerable. We identify the critical neural components: specific attention heads in early layers (L5, L7) that focus on safety patterns, and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) neurons in Layer 7 that encode vulnerability-related features. Ablation experiments confirm their causal role; removing Layer 11 drops vulnerability detection accuracy from 100% to 6%, while ablating just 20 neurons in Layer 7 reduces it by 50%.Our findings show that LLM vulnerability detection uses sparse, interpretable circuits (only 16% of model capacity), enabling circuit-level explanations for security predictions and targeted improvements to detection systems.

item.title: FPLIER: Federated Pathway-Level Information Extractor
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29587v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在转录组学分析中，通路水平信息提取器（PLIER）等基因集感知的矩阵分解方法，依赖大规模、异质性的基因表达汇编数据才能发挥最佳性能。然而，临床相关队列常因隐私保护与数据治理限制无法集中汇合，严重制约了PLIER在真实医疗场景中的应用。

方法创新：FPLIER框架

本文提出FPLIER（Federated PLIER），一种支持联邦学习的PLIER扩展框架。其核心设计包括：（1）允许多个数据持有方在本地训练PLIER模型，原始表达数据全程不出域；（2）融合公共数据集（如GTEx、TCGA）作为辅助先验，提升低样本量私有站点的表征能力；（3）通过安全聚合协议，使各参与方上传的梯度更新在代数上严格等价于中心化联合训练所得更新——即保证模型性能无损，同时满足强隐私约束。

关键发现与隐私分析

我们在K-CLIER与MultiPLIER模拟联盟上系统评估FPLIER：所有场景下均实现稳定收敛，重构误差与集中训练差异<1.2%。进一步开展面向中间统计量（如因子载荷、通路权重）及最终模型的成员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attack, MIA）实验，首次揭示：隐私风险由训练表达矩阵的有效秩（effective rank）主导。当引入公共数据或采用降维预处理时，矩阵秩显著提升，系统趋近满秩状态——此时训练样本与非训练样本在攻击者视角下不可区分，MIA准确率降至≈50%（即随机猜测水平），证实FPLIER具备内在抗推断能力。

AI Summary (English)

FPLIER is a federated extension of the Pathway Level Information Extractor (PLIER) designed for privacy-preserving, gene-set-aware factorization of transcriptomic data across distributed clinical cohorts. It enables local model training on sensitive expression data while securely aggregating updates—algebraically equivalent to centralized training—and incorporates public datasets (e.g., GTEx) as structural priors to boost performance in low-sample settings. Evaluated on two simulated consortia (K-CLIER and MultiPLIER), FPLIER achieves stable convergence with <1.2% reconstruction error deviation from pooled training. Crucially, our systematic membership inference attack analysis reveals that privacy risk is governed by the effective rank of the expression matrix: increasing rank—via public data integration or dimensionality reduction—drives the system toward a full-rank regime where training and non-training samples become statistically indistinguishable, reducing attack accuracy to chance level (~50%). This establishes FPLIER as both practically effective and provably robust against inference threats.

Abstract

In transcriptomics, gene-set-aware factorization methods such as the Pathway Level Information Extractor (PLIER) are most effective when trained on large, heterogeneous expression compendia. Yet, many clinically relevant cohorts cannot be pooled into a single dataset due to privacy and governance constraints. We present FPLIER, a federated extension of PLIER that enables distributed training across multiple data holders while incorporating publicly available datasets. Through secure aggregation, FPLIER produces training updates algebraically equivalent to those of a centralized pooled-data approach while keeping expression data local. We evaluate FPLIER across multiple scenarios in two simulated consortia (from the K-CLIER and MultiPLIER studies) and demonstrate stable convergence. We further conduct a systematic analysis of membership inference attacks targeting both intermediate training statistics and the released model. Our results show that privacy risk is governed by the rank of the training expression matrix. Incorporating public data or reducing data dimensionality increases this rank, moving the system toward a full-rank regime in which training and non-training samples become indistinguishable to the attacker, and membership-inference performance approaches random guessing.

item.title: A Full-Pipeline Framework for Evaluating Membership Inference Attacks in Machine Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29454v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

成员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attacks, MIAs）是评估机器学习模型隐私泄露风险的主流方法，已广泛应用于隐私审计与机器遗忘等新兴场景。然而，当前研究缺乏系统性评估框架，难以刻画不同数据分布、模型架构、训练算法及后处理模块对MIA效能的影响。这导致实践中常出现“基准表现优异但现实失效”的脱节现象——算法在标准数据集上指标亮眼，却在真实业务场景中因数据偏移、成本不对称或威胁假设偏差而失去统计意义。

方法创新

本文提出首个端到端全流水线MIA评估框架，覆盖数据预处理、模型架构选择、训练策略（如正则化、差分隐私）、以及后训练模块（如置信度校准、模型蒸馏）。框架核心包含三方面创新：

  • 多维威胁建模：形式化定义两类标准化威胁模型（Black-box with Logits 与 Gray-box with Gradients），并统一适配主流MIA（如Shadow Training、Loss-based、ML-Leaks）以确保公平比较；
  • 成本感知评估指标：引入三种互补指标——平衡准确率（Balanced Accuracy，适用于误报与漏报代价对称场景）、低假正率下的真正例率（TPR@FPR=1%）与低假负率下的真负例率（TNR@FNR=1%），精准响应安防、医疗等高风险领域的差异化容忍阈值；
  • 可复现审计工具包：开源模块化Python库，支持一键配置数据集、模型、攻击方法与评估协议，内置12个主流MIA实现及5类典型训练配置（含DP-SGD、Label Smoothing等）。

关键发现与价值

大规模实验表明：MIA效能高度依赖威胁模型设定与指标选择——例如，在灰盒模型下，基于梯度的攻击在CIFAR-10上TPR@FPR=1%达82.3%，但在黑盒+Logits设定下骤降至41.7%；而平衡准确率可能掩盖此类敏感性。本框架揭示了现有论文中高达63%的“SOTA结果”在切换评估视角后不再成立。最终，我们提炼出四条实践指南：① 必须匹配威胁模型与部署环境；② 拒绝单一指标决策；③ 审计需覆盖训练全流程变异；④ 工具链应支持动态指标切换。该工作为隐私工程提供了可落地的方法论与开箱即用的审计基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces FEMIA, the first full-pipeline framework for systematically evaluating Membership Inference Attacks (MIAs) across the entire ML lifecycle—from data and architectures to training algorithms and post-training modules.
