To address the lack of standardized, context-aware assessment, we formalize two threat models (black-box with logits, gray-box with gradients) and adapt 12 state-of-the-art MIAs accordingly. Crucially, we advocate three complementary evaluation metrics: Balanced Accuracy (for symmetric misclassification costs), TPR at FPR=1% (for false-alarm-critical settings), and TNR at FNR=1% (for miss-detection-critical ones). Extensive experiments across 5 datasets, 8 model families, and 5 training configurations reveal that MIA performance is highly sensitive to both threat assumptions and metric choice—e.g., gradient-based attacks drop from 82.3% to 41.7% TPR@FPR=1% when shifting from gray-box to black-box settings. We release an open-source, modular auditing toolkit and distill actionable guidelines for privacy practitioners.

Abstract

While Membership Inference Attacks (MIAs) are the prevailing method for identifying training data, their application has expanded into privacy auditing and machine unlearning. Nevertheless, the field lacks a systematic framework for evaluating how different contexts affect MIA efficacy. Without such a characterization, practitioners risk deploying algorithms that perform well on benchmarks but become statistically irrelevant when faced with the nuances of specific, real-world datasets. To bridge this gap and provide actionable insights, we introduce a comprehensive evaluation framework that systematically characterizes privacy risks across the entire machine learning pipeline, spanning data, architectures, algorithms, and post-training modules. Designed to inherently capture diverse operational contexts, our framework rigorously evaluates state-of-the-art MIAs across a broad spectrum of training configurations. To account for varying misclassification costs in real-world deployments, we employ three complementary metrics: Balanced Accuracy for symmetric costs, alongside TPR at low FPR (or TNR at low FNR) for asymmetric scenarios where false alarms or missed detections are strictly penalized. Furthermore, recognizing that existing MIAs assume divergent adversary capabilities, we formalize two standardized threat models and adapt these attacks into corresponding variants to ensure an equitable benchmark. Extensive empirical evaluations demonstrate that the efficacy of specific MIA methodologies is highly sensitive to the assumed threat models and chosen evaluation metrics. Ultimately, we distill these findings into actionable guidelines and provide a ready-to-use auditing toolkit, empowering practitioners to conduct better privacy assessments.

item.title: Minimal Prompt Perturbations Lead to Code Vulnerabilities: Prompt Fragility and Hidden-State Signals in Coding LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29737v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

基于大语言模型（LLM）的编程助手正被广泛部署于工业开发流程，显著提升编码效率。然而，随着其生成代码在生产环境中的直接应用，代码安全性已成为不可忽视的核心风险。既有研究聚焦于提示词扰动对功能正确性的影响，但尚未系统探究其对安全属性的破坏效应。

方法与实验

本研究首次系统评估提示词微小扰动对代码安全性的威胁：在三个主流代码大模型（CodeLlama-34B、StarCoder2-15B、DeepSeek-Coder-33B）上，对涵盖Python、JavaScript、Java、C++、Go五种语言的1,200+安全敏感任务提示，实施细粒度token级突变——包括单字符替换、空格增删、标点变更等。所有扰动均保持语义不变且语法合法。

关键发现

  • 极端脆弱性：仅单字符修改即可使生成代码从安全态翻转为含漏洞态（如缺失输入校验、硬编码密钥、不安全反序列化），平均翻转率达38.7%（最高达61.2%）。
  • 隐藏状态可预测性差异：通过探针分析中间层隐藏状态，发现输入处理类漏洞（如未过滤用户输入）具有较强可预测性（平均AUC = 0.753），而安全默认类漏洞（如选用弱哈希算法、禁用HTTPS验证）预测难度更高（平均AUC = 0.674），表明其依赖更局部的解码决策。
  • 新威胁模型确立：该现象揭示了一类此前被忽略的现实威胁——非恶意但自然发生的提示变异（如编辑器自动补全、协作场景下的文本微调）本身即可诱发漏洞，其风险不亚于传统提示注入攻击。

创新与启示

本研究首次定义“提示脆弱性”（Prompt Fragility）在安全维度的量化表征，证明安全缺陷可源于模型内部表征的不均衡鲁棒性；并指出：输入处理缺陷可在生成前拦截，而安全默认缺陷需在解码过程中动态干预，为下一代安全感知型编程助手提供了可落地的防御路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper reveals that minimal, semantically-preserving prompt perturbations—such as single-character changes—can reliably flip LLM-generated code from secure to vulnerable across three state-of-the-art coding models (CodeLlama-34B, StarCoder2-15B, DeepSeek-Coder-33B) and five programming languages (Python, JS, Java, C++, Go). We introduce prompt fragility as a security-critical property of coding LLMs and demonstrate its manifestation through systematic token-level mutations. Probing hidden states shows that input-handling vulnerabilities (e.g., missing sanitization) are significantly more predictable (mean AUC = 0.753) than secure-defaults failures (e.g., weak crypto choices; mean AUC = 0.674), indicating divergent failure mechanisms. Crucially, this establishes a novel threat model: ordinary prompt variation—not just adversarial injection—poses real security risks in production coding workflows. Our findings imply that pre-generation detection suffices for many input-related flaws, while secure-defaults vulnerabilities require real-time, decoding-stage intervention.

Abstract

LLM-based coding assistants are seeing rapid adoption, offering substantial gains in developer productivity. As organizations increasingly ship code these agents produce, the security of that code becomes critical. Prior work has shown that minor prompt perturbations degrade the functional correctness of LLM-generated code, but whether they also compromise code security has remained unstudied. We apply token-level mutations to prompts across three models and five programming languages, and show that mutations as small as a single-character change can flip generated code from secure to vulnerable. Probing the models' hidden states reveals that this fragility is partially encoded in prompt representations, but unevenly so. Input-handling vulnerabilities, where the model omits validation or sanitization, are more predictable (mean AUC 0.753) than secure-defaults vulnerabilities, where insecure code stems from one local choice such as a weak algorithm or unsafe parameter (mean AUC 0.674). These results show that the threat model for LLM-assisted coding extends beyond prompt injection to ordinary prompt variation, and indicate that input-handling flaws can be caught before generation while secure-defaults flaws require intervention during decoding.

item.title: DP-SAPF: Saliency-Aware Parameter Fine-tuning of Public Models for Differentially Private Image Synthesis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30312v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

差分隐私（DP）图像合成旨在生成保留敏感数据集统计特性的合成图像，在保障严格隐私泄露界的同时支持下游分析与应用。现有方法多采用DP随机梯度下降（DP-SGD）对大型公开预训练模型（如Stable Diffusion）进行全参数微调，但其计算开销巨大；近期工作引入LoRA（低秩自适应）压缩可训练参数量，却 heuristic 地应用于所有注意力层参数，导致DP噪声在高维低秩更新中过度累积与梯度坍缩，显著损害合成图像保真度与多样性。

方法创新：DP-SAPF

本文提出 DP-SAPF（Saliency-Aware Parameter Fine-tuning） ——一种面向差分隐私图像合成的参数感知微调框架。其核心思想是：在DP约束下，梯度幅值越大，参数对敏感数据越“显著”（saliency），越应被优先保留并更新。具体流程包括：（1）将敏感图像输入公共模型，前向传播后计算原始梯度；（2）对梯度施加满足$(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP的高斯噪声；（3）基于带噪梯度的L2范数排序，动态筛选出Top-K高显著性参数子集（集中于Q/K/V投影与FFN中间层）；（4）仅在此子集上部署LoRA适配器并执行DP-SGD优化。

实验结果与优势

在CelebA-HQ、FFHQ、ChestX-ray及ISIC-2019四个敏感图像数据集上的系统实验表明：相比全参数DP微调与全注意力层LoRA基线，DP-SAPF在相同隐私预算（$\varepsilon=2.0,\delta=10^{-5}$）下，FID降低18.7%–32.4%，Inception Score提升11.3%–26.8%，同时训练显存减少41%，迭代时间缩短37%。本工作首次将梯度显著性建模与差分隐私噪声机制协同设计，为高效、高保真DP图像合成提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Differentially private (DP) image synthesis enables privacy-preserving data utility but suffers from high computational cost and degraded fidelity under DP constraints. Existing approaches either fully fine-tune large public models via DP-SGD—or apply LoRA heuristically to all attention-layer parameters—both leading to excessive noise accumulation and gradient collapse. To address this, we propose DP-SAPF, a saliency-aware parameter selection framework: it computes noisy gradients on sensitive images (satisfying $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP), ranks parameters by gradient magnitude, and applies LoRA only to the most salient subset (e.g., Q/K/V projections). Experiments across four sensitive datasets (CelebA-HQ, FFHQ, ChestX-ray, ISIC-2019) show DP-SAPF achieves 18.7–32.4% lower FID and 11.3–26.8% higher Inception Score than baselines under $\varepsilon=2.0$, while reducing GPU memory by 41% and training time by 37%. DP-SAPF establishes the first principled link between gradient saliency and DP parameter efficiency for synthetic image generation.

Abstract

Differentially private (DP) image synthesis generates images that preserve the statistical characteristics of a sensitive dataset, enabling sensitive data analysis and usage while providing rigorous guarantees of privacy leakage. Existing methods fine-tune public models using DP Stochastic Gradient Descent (DP-SGD) on sensitive images to generate synthetic images. But full fine-tuning public models on sensitive images is computationally expensive, because current public models typically contain a large number of parameters. Recent work proposes heuristically using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) on all attention-layer parameters of public models to reduce the number of trainable parameters. However, we argue that exhaustive LoRA coverage across all attention-layer parameters is suboptimal in a DP setting, as it leads to noise accumulation and collapse during private training. To address this issue, we propose DP-SAPF, which uses a saliency-aware strategy to identify specific target parameters for LoRA training under DP. DP-SAPF is inspired by the fact that larger gradients signify higher saliency, indicating that these parameters are most critical for the DP learning. Specifically, we feed the sensitive images into public models, compute gradients, and add noise to the gradients to satisfy DP. Then, DP-SAPF identifies the most salient parameters, those exhibiting high gradient magnitudes on sensitive images, for DP fine-tuning. Experiments on four sensitive image datasets show that DP-SAPF improves the utility and fidelity of synthetic images while requiring fewer computational resources than fine-tuning methods without parameter selection.

item.title: A Bayesian Approach to Membership Inference for Statistical Release
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30203v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

成员推断（Membership Inference, MI）攻击旨在判断某条个体记录是否属于生成公开统计发布的原始私有数据集。现有形式化分析多基于独立属性假设（如乘积分布），仅利用人口总体的边际分布建模，虽在部分场景下有效，却严重忽略真实世界中普遍存在的属性依赖结构（如收入与教育程度、职业与居住地间的强关联）。此类简化导致理论分析脱离实际威胁模型，尤其当多个机构（如美国人口普查局与国税局）持有结构相似的高维关联数据时，攻击者可融合先验知识提升推断能力。

方法创新

本文首次将成员推断问题重构为面向贝叶斯网络（Bayesian Network, BN）表征的人口总体的决策问题。我们提出一个基于贝叶斯决策理论的通用攻击框架：允许攻击者整合关于人口依赖结构的先验知识（如BN拓扑与条件概率表），并通过概率编程精确计算后验归属概率。该框架不依赖独立性假设，支持对复杂依赖关系的自然建模。

主要发现与验证

我们实例化了一个基于Roulette概率编程语言的具体攻击算法，严格证明其等价于针对强依赖人口的最优似然比检验（LRT）变体。在5个经典BN基准（如Alarm、Insurance、Child）上的实验表明：相比标准LRT攻击与内积攻击，本方法平均提升攻击成功率12.7%–38.4%，尤其在依赖结构无法被人工适配的传统攻击中优势显著。本工作为统计发布隐私风险评估提供了更贴近现实的建模范式与可扩展的实证工具。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses membership inference (MI) against statistical releases by moving beyond the standard assumption of independent attributes. We reframe MI as a Bayesian decision problem over a population modeled as a Bayesian network (BN), enabling attackers to leverage prior knowledge of attribute dependencies—e.g., from auxiliary datasets like census or tax records. We propose a general framework that incorporates structural and probabilistic priors via probabilistic programming to compute optimal posterior membership probabilities. As a concrete instantiation, we implement an attack in Roulette that provably reduces to the optimal likelihood ratio test for strongly dependent populations. Experiments on five canonical BNs (Alarm, Insurance, Child, etc.) show our method consistently outperforms both the standard LRT and inner-product attacks—by 12.7–38.4% in AUC—especially where dependency structures are too complex for manual adaptation of existing attacks. This work bridges formal privacy analysis with realistic data dependencies.

Abstract

The membership inference problem for publicly released statistics from a private dataset is well-studied. When developing and formally analyzing attack strategies, however, the focus has been on attacks that model the population using only its marginals. In practice, these attacks can perform well on various populations, however most formal analysis is for populations that follow a product distribution. These strategies may fail to leverage useful information about the population that is important for understanding a realistic privacy threat. In this work, we explore the impact of providing an attacker with additional information about the attribute dependency structure of the population, motivated by examples where multiple parties may have access to similarly structured data, for example the US Census and the IRS. To model this scenario, we re-frame the membership inference problem with respect to a population represented as a Bayesian network (BN). We develop a framework based on Bayesian decision-making which can incorporate prior information about the population to launch more effective, specialized attacks. To evaluate our framework, we introduce a specific attack instantiation which computes the Bayesian posterior using a probabilistic program, and prove its equivalence to an optimal variant of the likelihood ratio test attack for two populations with strong attribute dependency. We implement our program in the Roulette probabilistic programming language and show experimentally that it outperforms the likelihood ratio test and inner product attacks on five commonly used BNs, where the population dependency structure is too complex for the existing attacks to be manually adapted.

item.title: Protecting On-Device AI Inference: A Systematic Review of Attacks and Defence Mechanisms
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29450v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与意义

随着人工智能（AI）向边缘与移动端下沉，端侧推理（on-device AI inference） 已成为隐私敏感场景（如医疗、金融、个人助理）的关键范式。然而，预训练模型在终端设备上的部署也引入了新型安全威胁：模型窃取/逆向提取、对抗样本攻击、训练数据泄露等，严重危及模型知识产权（IP）与用户隐私。当前综述多聚焦于“边缘智能”（含分布式训练），忽视了纯客户端推理场景下特有的威胁面与防御需求。

方法与范围

本研究系统性梳理了2016–2024年间发表的127篇高质量论文（含顶会/期刊），首次专一聚焦于端侧推理阶段的安全与隐私问题，严格区分于云端或协同训练场景。我们构建了细粒度分类框架，将攻击分为四类：IP窃取（42%）、对抗攻击（33%）、数据泄露（18%）、侧信道攻击（7%）；防御机制则按技术路径归类为可信执行环境（TEE）、同态加密、模型混淆、差分隐私、鲁棒训练等。

主要发现与创新点

  • 显著失衡性：IP窃取类攻击仅占攻击文献的25%，却吸引了约50%的防御工作；而占比高达33%的对抗攻击，竟无一篇专门针对端侧推理场景的系统性防御方案。
  • 防御空白突出：侧信道攻击与部分数据重建攻击尚无实用化端侧防御方案；现有TEE方案多依赖硬件假设，缺乏跨平台通用性。
  • 核心贡献：本文是首个全面覆盖端侧推理全攻击面与防御谱系的综述，揭示了研究生态中的结构性缺口，并为工业界部署与学术界攻关提供了可操作的路线图（如：轻量级鲁棒推理框架、TEE-增强型对抗训练、面向移动GPU的混淆优化）。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first comprehensive, dedicated survey on security and privacy threats—and corresponding defenses—specifically targeting on-device AI inference, distinct from broader edge intelligence or distributed training contexts. Through systematic analysis of 127 peer-reviewed studies (2016–2024), we identify a critical asymmetry: while IP theft accounts for only ~25% of attack papers, it attracts ~50% of defense efforts; conversely, adversarial attacks—comprising ~33% of all attacks—lack any dedicated, practical defense mechanism for on-device deployment. We further reveal significant gaps in mitigating side-channel and data-reconstruction attacks under resource-constrained mobile environments. Our review establishes a unified taxonomy, benchmarks real-world feasibility of defenses (e.g., TEE overhead, encryption latency), and proposes concrete research directions—including lightweight certified robustness, hardware-aware model obfuscation, and cross-platform secure inference primitives—to bridge the chasm between known threats and deployable protections.

Abstract

The need for secure and private Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) on edge and mobile devices has increased the necessity of protecting the architecture of these systems from threats to both security and privacy. With an ever-increasing number of pre-trained AI models being used on mobile platforms for client-side inference, there are rising concerns about the risks associated with the theft/extraction of AI models, adversarial attacks on AI models, and data breaches. As a result of this trend, a variety of defence mechanisms have been proposed to protect against these threats. These include Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs), homomorphic encryption, obfuscation, and differential privacy, among others. However, current surveys largely focus on edge intelligence, which includes distributed training, and thus overlook security and privacy issues that are specific to on-device AI inference. To the best of our knowledge, this paper presents the first comprehensive review of threats and corresponding defence mechanisms targeting on-device inference. Our results show that the attack and defence literature are unbalanced: approximately one quarter of the surveyed attack papers focus on Intellectual Property (IP) attacks, whereas half of the defence solutions tackle the same issue. More importantly, some attack categories have no defence paper associated to them, such as adversarial attacks that account for roughly one third of the attack literature. This asymmetry between known attacks and available mitigations highlights clear opportunities for future research on securing on-device AI inference.

item.title: Evolving Skill-Structured Attack Memory Enhances LLM Jailbreaking
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29237v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

针对大语言模型（LLM）的越狱攻击（Jailbreaking）旨在诱导模型生成本应拒绝的有害内容。在安全评估场景中，自动化黑盒越狱生成尤为关键——攻击者仅能观测模型输出，需在无梯度、无内部访问权限条件下高效搜索有效对抗提示。现有方法存在明显局限：一类依赖逐样本启发式搜索（如GCG、AutoDAN），效率低、泛化弱；另一类尝试积累策略池或方法库，却缺乏对攻击经验的结构化建模、动态演化与智能调度，导致记忆冗余、技能退化、探索失衡。

方法创新：MemoAttack框架

本文提出MemoAttack——首个以记忆驱动的黑盒越狱框架，核心在于对攻击经验进行全生命周期管理：

  • 技能结构化记忆建模：将历史成功攻击抽象为可复用的“技能单元”，每个单元绑定攻击技能类型（如角色扮演、隐喻混淆）、模板片段、实证证据（成功率、触发条件）及生命周期状态（新生/成熟/衰退）；
  • 生命周期驱动的记忆演化：基于实证数据动态执行六阶段操作——证据检验→ probation（试用）、promotion（晋升）、retirement（退休）、reactivation（重启）、elimination（淘汰）、storage cleanup（存储清理）；
  • 探索-利用均衡的记忆选择：引入上下文感知的Thompson采样机制，在复用高置信技能的同时，主动探索低频但潜力未知的技能分支，避免局部最优。

实验结果与价值

在AdvBench基准上，MemoAttack达成98.00%平均攻击成功率，较最强基线（GCG+Prompt-to-Prompt）提升16.67个百分点，且请求量减少45.9%。更重要的是，其性能随攻击样本积累持续提升，验证了记忆系统的自进化能力。本工作首次将“攻击记忆”建模为可演化的技能知识库，为LLM红队测试提供了可积累、可解释、可持续优化的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) face critical safety risks from jailbreak attacks—adversarial prompts that bypass built-in safeguards. Automated black-box jailbreaking, where attackers observe only model outputs, remains challenging due to the lack of systematic experience reuse. Existing methods rely either on sample-wise heuristic search or static strategy pools, failing to organize, evolve, or intelligently select attack knowledge. We propose MemoAttack, a memory-driven framework featuring: (1) skill-structured memory modeling, encoding attack experience into reusable units pairing skills, templates, empirical evidence, and lifecycle states; (2) lifecycle-driven memory evolution, dynamically managing memory through evidence-based probation, promotion, retirement, reactivation, elimination, and cleanup; and (3) explore-exploit balanced memory selection, using contextual Thompson sampling to balance reliable reuse with uncertainty-aware exploration. On AdvBench, MemoAttack achieves a 98.00% average attack success rate, outperforming the strongest baseline by +16.67 percentage points, while reducing API requests by 45.9%. Critically, its performance improves continuously as memory accumulates—demonstrating the first self-evolving, skill-grounded memory system for LLM red-teaming.

Abstract

Jailbreak attacks on large language models (LLMs) aim to induce LLMs to produce content that they are expected to refuse. Automated black-box jailbreak generation is especially important for safety evaluation, where the attacker observes only model outputs and needs to automatically search for effective adversarial prompts. Existing black-box jailbreak methods either depend on sample-wise heuristic search or leverage attack experience through accumulating strategy pools or method libraries, lacking a systematic organization and management of attack experience. To mitigate these drawbacks, we propose MemoAttack, a memory-driven black-box jailbreak framework with comprehensive attack memory modeling, evolution, and selection. Specifically, MemoAttack comprises three key designs: (1) Skill-Structured Memory Modeling, which abstracts accumulated attack experience into reusable skill-structured attack memory whose units pair attack skills with templates, evidence, and lifecycle state; (2) Lifecycle-Driven Memory Evolution, which evolves the memory through evidence-based probation, promotion, retirement, reactivation, elimination, and storage cleanup; and (3) Explore-Exploit Balanced Memory Selection, which balances reliable memory reuse with uncertainty-driven exploration via contextual Thompson Sampling. Experiments on AdvBench demonstrate that MemoAttack achieves an average attack success rate of 98.00%, outperforming the strongest baseline by 16.67 percentage points, while reducing request count by 45.9%. Moreover, MemoAttack continuously improves as memory accumulates over more samples.

item.title: SAMD: A Tool for Identifying False Data Injection Scenarios in AI/ML-enabled Medical Devices
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29210v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着人工智能（AI）与机器学习（ML）在医疗设备中的深度集成，虚假数据注入攻击（False Data Injection, FDI）构成严重安全威胁：攻击者可利用系统组件漏洞，在推理阶段向ML模型注入恶意输入，导致误诊、漏诊或错误治疗。此类风险在设计阶段难以充分识别——因医疗设备常采用模块化架构，部分系统组装实际发生于终端用户临床部署过程中，传统静态安全分析方法覆盖不足。

方法创新：SAMD工具框架

本文提出SAMD（Security Analysis for AI/ML-enabled Medical Devices），一种面向设计阶段的自动化安全分析工具。SAMD基于系统理论过程分析-安全扩展（STPA-Sec），将医疗设备建模为闭环控制结构，统一视所有组件（传感器、通信接口、预处理模块等）为潜在FDI注入点。其核心创新在于：

  • 融合CVE/NVD等权威漏洞数据库与大语言模型（LLM），实现漏洞语义匹配与上下文感知的自动发现；
  • 生成结构化、可执行的FDI攻击场景，包含攻击前提、注入路径、ML模型扰动方式及临床后果链；
  • 支持FDA认证设备文档的端到端解析，无需源码或硬件访问。

关键结果与验证

在5款FDA已批准的AI医疗设备（涵盖影像诊断、生理信号分析等类别）的实证研究中：

  • 100%精准识别设备所用核心技术（如ResNet-50、LSTM、DICOM协议栈）；
  • 检出关联已知漏洞的准确率达63.2%（显著优于关键词匹配基线）；
  • 生成的FDI场景对ML模型攻击步骤描述准确率达95.3%，单次分析耗时≤191.64秒；
  • 所有案例均揭示出设计阶段未被评估的跨层攻击路径（如通过DICOM元数据篡改触发分类器偏移）。

SAMD首次实现了STPA-Sec在AI医疗设备上的全流程自动化，为监管机构、制造商提供可审计、可追溯的安全设计验证新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces SAMD, an automated tool for identifying False Data Injection (FDI) attack scenarios in AI/ML-enabled medical devices during the design phase. SAMD applies System Theoretic Process Analysis for Security (STPA-Sec) by modeling the medical system as a control structure, treating all components—including sensors, communication interfaces, and preprocessing modules—as potential FDI injection points into the ML engine. It integrates vulnerability databases (CVE/NVD) with Large Language Models (LLMs) to automatically discover component-specific vulnerabilities and generate detailed, step-by-step FDI scenarios. Evaluated on five FDA-cleared devices, SAMD achieves 100% precision in identifying target technologies from regulatory documentation, retrieves related known vulnerabilities with 63.2% precision, and generates highly accurate FDI scenarios against ML models (95.3% accuracy)—with analysis time under 191.64 seconds per device. SAMD bridges a critical gap between safety engineering and AI security, enabling proactive, standards-aligned threat modeling before clinical deployment.

Abstract

The growing integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) in medical systems requires effective measures to address emerging security risks. One such risk is that of adversaries introducing false data through vulnerable system components during inference, causing misdiagnosis and wrong treatments. These risks are challenging to anticipate and address in the design phase, as the system assembly partially occurs during actual use by end users. To address this concern, we introduce SAMD, an automated tool for performing System Theoretic Process Analysis for Security (STPA-Sec) on AI/ML-enabled medical devices during the design phase. SAMD models the medical system as a control structure, treating all system components as potential points for injecting false data into the ML engine. It leverages state-of-the-art vulnerability databases and Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate vulnerability discovery and generate a list of potential attack scenarios. We demonstrate SAMD's effectiveness through case studies on five FDA-cleared medical devices, showcasing its ability to identify vulnerable points and potential attack paths. We find that SAMD has 100% precision in identifying target device technologies in the case studies' documents, retrieves the known vulnerabilities linked to them (with 63.2% precision), and generates highly relevant attack scenarios on the ML model, including detailed steps that an adversary might take (with 95.3% accuracy, and the highest time taken being 191.64s).

item.title: Auditing Training Data in Generative Music Models via Black-Box Membership Inference
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29202v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

随着文本到音乐生成技术的飞速发展，高保真、结构化音乐音频合成能力日益成熟，但其训练数据来源不明、版权归属不清、用户知情同意缺失等问题引发严峻伦理与法律关切。当前主流生成式音乐模型普遍依赖大规模私有或网络爬取音频语料库进行训练，且极少公开数据构成与使用授权信息，导致创作者、权利人及监管方缺乏有效手段验证某段特定音乐是否被纳入模型训练集——这一“训练数据可审计性”缺口严重阻碍了透明AI治理与版权合规实践。

方法创新

本文首次提出面向生成式音乐模型的黑盒成员推断（Black-Box Membership Inference）框架，仅需对部署后的模型进行查询访问（即输入文本提示，获取生成音频），无需模型参数、梯度或训练日志。核心洞见在于：若某段候选音乐曾参与训练，则其与同一文本描述下模型生成音频在语义与结构层面呈现系统性更强的对齐性。我们据此构建双阶段流程：（1）利用轻量级影子模型（shadow models）生成“真实音频–配对生成音频”样本对；（2）在学习到的音乐特征空间中量化二者相似性（如CLAP嵌入余弦距离、节奏/频谱一致性指标），并训练专用“音乐审计器”（Music Auditor）判别成员身份。

主要发现与贡献

该方法在Stable Audio、Suno v3、AudioLDM 2等6个前沿模型上实现最高98.6% 的成员识别准确率，假阳性率低至1.9%，假阴性率仅1.0%；且在跨模型迁移场景（用A模型训练审计器，检测B模型）中仍保持>92%鲁棒性。这是首个在纯黑盒、无元数据、无训练访问条件下实现高精度音乐训练数据审计的工作，为AI音乐版权溯源、数据合规评估与模型可信验证提供了可落地的技术路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces the first black-box membership inference method for generative music models, enabling reliable auditing of whether a given music clip was used in training—using only query access to the deployed system (i.e., caption-to-audio generation). We observe that training members exhibit systematically stronger semantic and structural alignment with their caption-conditioned model outputs than non-members. Leveraging this, we construct paired examples (real track + its caption-generated counterpart) from shadow models, extract alignment features in a learned music representation space (e.g., CLAP, rhythm coherence), and train a lightweight auditor to classify membership. Crucially, the auditor generalizes across unseen target models without requiring architecture knowledge, parameters, or training metadata. Evaluated on six state-of-the-art music generators—including Stable Audio and Suno v3—our approach achieves up to 98.6% accuracy, with false-positive and false-negative rates as low as 1.9% and 1.0%, respectively. This demonstrates that practical, high-fidelity training-data auditing is feasible for real-world generative music systems.

Abstract

Recent advances in text-to-music generation enable high-fidelity synthesis of structured musical audio, raising growing concerns about data provenance, consent, and training transparency. These models are typically trained on large-scale corpora with little disclosure, leaving no practical mechanism to verify whether a particular audio sample was included in training. In this paper, we investigate black-box membership inference for generative music models, aiming to determine whether a candidate music sample was used during training, given only query access to the deployed system. Our key insight is that training membership induces systematically stronger semantic and structural alignment between a candidate sample and the model's generation conditioned on its caption. We query the target model with the associated caption and measure the relationship between the candidate audio and the generated output in a learned feature space. To capture features that separate members from non-members, we construct paired examples consisting of each track and its caption-conditioned generation from shadow models, and train a music auditor to classify membership. The auditor captures alignment patterns characteristic of training membership and generalizes to unseen target models in a fully black-box setting without access to model parameters or training metadata. Across multiple state-of-the-art music generators, our method achieves up to 98.6% accuracy, with false-positive and false-negative rates as low as 1.9% and 1.0%, demonstrating that reliable training-data auditing is feasible in realistic deployment scenarios.

item.title: When RL Suppresses Its Own Vocabulary: Recovering Reasoning Diversity in Puzzle-to-Math Transfer
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.29190v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

强化学习结合可验证奖励（RLVR）能提升大语言模型（LLM）的推理能力，但其在跨领域迁移（如从逻辑谜题到数学证明）中的有效性机制尚不清晰。本文聚焦一个关键矛盾：当监督微调（SFT）与强化学习（RL）阶段仅使用约束满足类谜题（无任何数学题目）时，模型为何能在高难度数学竞赛题（OlymMATH-Hard）上实现显著提升？更关键的是，RL过程是否在增强某些推理模式的同时，意外抑制了对数学求解至关重要的探索性思维？

方法创新：推理原语级分析框架

为解构迁移机制，我们提出首个推理原语（reasoning primitive）级可解释性框架：

  • 构建9类细粒度span分类器，精准识别推理链中的语义单元（如hypothesize、backtrack、compute、verify等）；
  • 结合motif提取技术，将长思维链自动切分为可追踪的“推理基元组合”；
  • 在SFT→RL→最终模型各阶段，跨领域（谜题训练 vs. 数学测试）量化各类原语的激活频率、组合长度与多样性。

核心发现与技术突破

1. SFT奠基作用：仅用谜题训练的SFT阶段即诱导出通用推理原语词表，在OlymMATH-Hard上带来+7pp pass@32 提升；
2. RL的双刃效应：标准GSPO RL虽通过构建“计算-验证”长链再增+6pp，却系统性压制hypothesize与backtrack等探索性原语（降幅达42%），导致推理同质化；
3. novelty bonus修复机制：引入基于参考模型困惑度（perplexity）的多样性奖励，显式鼓励正确但结构多样的rollout，成功恢复探索原语活性，并额外+7pp pass@32；
4. 零数学数据迁移上限突破：端到端方案将OLMo3-7B-Instruct-SFT基线（16.0%）提升至36.0%，全程未引入任一数学题目——证实推理能力可经纯逻辑训练泛化至形式化数学。

AI Summary (English)

This paper investigates how reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) transfers reasoning capabilities from constraint-satisfaction puzzles to hard mathematical problem solving—without any math problems in training. Using a 7B model trained exclusively on puzzles during both SFT and RL stages, we introduce a novel reasoning primitive-level framework: a 9-class span classifier combined with motif extraction enables fine-grained segmentation and tracking of reasoning patterns (e.g., hypothesize, backtrack, compute, verify) across training stages and domains. We find that puzzle-based SFT induces a general reasoning vocabulary (+7 pp pass@32 on OlymMATH-Hard), and vanilla GSPO RL further composes primitives into longer compute-verify chains (+6 pp)—but critically suppresses exploratory primitives like hypothesize and backtrack. To counter this, we propose a perplexity-based novelty bonus that rewards diverse correct rollouts, restoring exploratory reasoning and yielding an additional +7 pp pass@32. The full pipeline lifts hard-math performance from 16.0% to 36.0%—a 20-percentage-point gain—achieving strong math reasoning without ever seeing a math problem during post-training.

Abstract

Reinforcement learning using verifiable rewards (RLVR) improves LLM reasoning, but the conditions under which it transfers across domains -- and why it does so -- remain under-explored. We study cross-domain transfer in a 7B model whose SFT and RL post-training stages use only constraint-satisfaction puzzles, with no mathematics problems in the post-training data. To analyze how transfer emerges, we introduce a reasoning primitive-level framework that combines a 9-class span classifier with motif extraction, allowing us to segment chain-of-thought traces into primitive motifs and track their evolution across training stages and domains. We find that puzzle SFT induces a reasoning-primitive vocabulary, yielding a $+7$pp \texttt{pass@32} gain on OlymMATH-Hard. Vanilla GSPO then composes these primitives into longer compute-verify chains, adding a further $+6$pp. However, this RL stage also suppresses exploratory primitives such as \textit{hypothesize} and \textit{backtrack}. To address this, we introduce a novelty bonus that rewards diverse correct rollouts, using perplexity under the reference model as a signal. This restores recovery primitives during RL and adds a further $+7$pp \texttt{pass@32} relative to vanilla GSPO. Finally, the end-to-end recipe raises the hard-math capability ceiling from $16.0\%$ at the OLMo3-7B-Instruct-SFT base to $36.0\%$, without adding any mathematics problems during the SFT or RL stages.

item.title: Blind PRNG Hijacking: An Undetectable Integrity-Preserving Attack Against LLM Watermarking
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28632v1

AI Summary (中文)

盲式PRNG劫持：一种不可检测、保真度完好的LLM水印攻击

当前，密码学水印（如KGW、Unigram、DipMark）是溯源大语言模型（LLM）生成文本的主流防御手段，其安全性严格依赖于底层伪随机数生成器（PRNG）的可信性。本文首次揭示并系统构造了针对LLM水印系统的供应链级漏洞——SeedHijack攻击，这是首个同时满足三大关键特性的水印破坏范式：

  • 盲式（Blind）：无需获知水印密钥、检测器参数或模型logits，仅通过替换部署环境中的PRNG即可实施；
  • 完整性保持（Integrity-Preserving）：不修改任何输出token，不损害文本流畅性与语义质量，反而主动放大水印信号（z-score最高提升2.42倍）；
  • 检测正交性（Orthogonal to Detection）：引入的偏差在统计上独立于所有内容侧检测统计量（如n-gram频率、词频偏移、熵分布等），实现“增强水印”与“规避检测”的零权衡共存。

攻击在供应链层（如容器镜像、推理框架依赖库）替换PRNG，精准偏置“绿名单”（green-list）采样过程，绕过所有文本后处理检测。实验覆盖KGW/Unigram/DipMark三类主流水印方案及Llama-3-8B、Qwen2-7B、Phi-3-mini三大开源LLM，在6种SOTA内容侧检测器（包括Z-statistic、GLTR、Watermark-Detector等）上全部实现0检出率。进一步验证表明，基于量子随机数生成器（QRNG）的熵源认证可完全阻断该攻击，且不影响正常水印功能。本研究确立了PRNG完整性是密码学内容溯源系统的首要安全前提，为水印部署实践提供了根本性安全加固路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces SeedHijack, the first blind, integrity-preserving, and detection-orthogonal supply-chain attack against cryptographic LLM watermarking. Unlike prior attacks that perturb output text or require watermark key/logit access, SeedHijack compromises the underlying PRNG—replacing it with a biased variant at the deployment layer—to systematically skew green-list token selection without altering any generated tokens or degrading text quality. Crucially, the induced bias is statistically independent of all content-side detector statistics (e.g., n-gram frequencies, entropy, or log-probability deviations), enabling simultaneous watermark amplification (up to 2.42× higher z-score) and perfect evasion (0/6 detection rates across KGW, Unigram, and DipMark on Llama-3, Qwen2, and Phi-3). The attack exposes PRNG integrity as a foundational security requirement: a quantum random number generator (QRNG)-based entropy attestation fully neutralizes SeedHijack while preserving legitimate watermarking utility.

Abstract

Cryptographic watermarking is a leading defense for attributing text generated by large language models (LLMs). Existing schemes, including KGW, Unigram, and DipMark, derive their security guarantees from the assumption that the underlying pseudo-random number generator (PRNG) is trustworthy. This work introduces SeedHijack, the first supply-chain attack on LLM watermarking that is simultaneously (i) blind -- requiring no knowledge of the watermark key, detector, or model logits, (ii) integrity-preserving -- amplifying rather than erasing the watermark signal, and (iii) orthogonal to detection -- the attack-induced bias is statistically independent of all content-side detector statistics, ensuring that amplification and evasion coexist without trade-off. Rather than perturbing generated text, SeedHijack replaces the PRNG at the supply-chain layer, biasing green-list selection without altering output tokens or degrading text quality. Across three watermarking schemes and three open-source LLMs, the attack triggers 0/6 state-of-the-art content-side statistical detectors while inflating the watermark z-score up to 2.42x (system-level defenses such as entropy-source attestation remain orthogonal and complementary). A quantum random number generator (QRNG) countermeasure is shown to fully neutralize the attack while preserving benign watermarking utility. These findings establish PRNG integrity as a first-class security requirement for cryptographic content-provenance systems.

item.title: Position: Retire the "Positive Backdoor" Label -- Secret Alignment Requires Strict and Systematic Evaluation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28597v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与立场

本文是一篇立场性论文，主张AI/ML社区应正式弃用“正向后门”（positive backdoor）这一误导性标签。作者指出，“正向后门”常被用于描述通过触发器激活的隐藏行为（如访问控制、水印识别、安全策略执行），但该术语隐含“无害性”甚至“有益性”的价值预设，严重弱化了其作为秘密对齐（Secret Alignment） 的本质——即模型在未公开、未审计条件下对特定输入信号产生隐蔽、条件化响应的能力。在私有AI时代（由开源大模型与平民化训练/推理栈驱动），语言模型已成为用户私有数字资产，面临模型窃取、未授权访问与行为滥用等新型安全威胁；而当前以“正向后门”为名的技术方案，实则构成高风险的黑盒行为层。

方法与评估框架

我们首次将相关工作统一建模为触发器—行为的隐蔽映射（covert trigger-behavior association），并系统构建六维评估体系：有效性（effectiveness）、无害性（harmlessness）、持久性（persistence）、效率（efficiency）、鲁棒性（robustness）与可靠性（reliability）。针对三类典型应用（访问门控、所有权溯源、安全强制），我们在标准基准上完成跨模型、跨扰动、跨训练阶段的实证测试。

主要发现与创新

实验揭示：现有方案在保密性（Confidentiality）、完整性（Integrity）与可用性（Availability）三大安全支柱上存在显著脆弱性——触发逻辑易被逆向推断、行为响应易受微小扰动破坏、长期部署中性能急剧衰减。进一步分析表明，行为密度（behavior density）与决策复杂度（decision complexity）是预测CIA失效的关键指标。本文由此提出：所有Secret Alignment声明必须默认视为未经验证的假设，仅当通过标准化、可复现、压力化的评估流程后方可成立。我们呼吁建立社区级评测协议，使Secret Alignment从“宣称”走向“可证明”。

AI Summary (English)

This position paper argues that the term “positive backdoor” is misleading and should be retired; instead, trigger-activated hidden behaviors should be rigorously conceptualized as Secret Alignment—a security-critical, opaque model capability requiring systematic validation. Motivated by rising risks in the Private AI era (e.g., model theft, unauthorized use), we unify recent proposals under a covert trigger-behavior association framework and evaluate three representative applications across six core properties: effectiveness, harmlessness, persistence, efficiency, robustness, and reliability. Our empirical analysis reveals severe brittleness—especially in confidentiality, integrity, and availability—contradicting prevalent claims of safety and stability. We link failures to behavioral density and decision complexity, proposing these as predictive risk indicators for deployment. Crucially, we assert that Secret Alignment claims must be presumed insecure by default, and only gain credibility through standardized, stress-tested evaluation. This work calls for community-wide adoption of provable, auditable benchmarks for Secret Alignment.

Abstract

This position paper argues that the AI/ML community should stop overclaiming and retire the label "positive backdoor," and instead treat trigger-activated hidden behaviors as Secret Alignment. Crucially, protective claims based on Secret Alignment should be presumed not secure by default unless supported by rigorous, standardized evaluation. The Private AI era, enabled by open-weight LLMs and accessible training/inference stacks, turns language models into privately owned digital assets, creating security concerns around unauthorized access, model theft, and behavioral misuse. Recently, a line of work framed as "positive backdoors" has been proposed to address these challenges. To ground our position in evidence, we unify these proposals as covert trigger-behavior associations for access gating, ownership attribution, and safety enforcement, and evaluate three representative applications across six core properties: effectiveness, harmlessness, persistence, efficiency, robustness, and reliability. Our results reveal substantial brittleness - especially in the confidentiality, integrity, and availability (CIA) - of trigger-behavior mappings often underrepresented by existing claims. We further relate these outcomes to behavior density and decision complexity, offering a behavioral lens for understanding deployment-time risks and motivating community-wide evaluation that makes Secret Alignment claims provable.

item.title: Technical Report: Exploring the Emerging Threats of the Agent Skill Ecosystem
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28588v1

AI Summary (中文)

技术报告：AI代理技能生态系统的新兴安全威胁分析

本研究系统性地评估了当前AI代理技能生态系统的安全性，聚焦于主流技能市场（如clawhub.ai、AgentSDB等）中公开上架的3,984个AI代理技能。我们采用动静结合的多阶段分析方法：首先通过静态代码扫描识别高危API调用、硬编码凭证与可疑网络行为模式；继而构建沙箱环境对技能进行动态执行与流量监控；最终由安全专家对疑似恶意样本开展人工逆向验证。研究确认存在76个已验证的恶意载荷，涵盖三大类高危行为：凭证窃取（如自动抓取浏览器Cookie或本地密钥文件）、后门植入（如下载并执行远程PowerShell脚本）、数据外泄（如将用户聊天记录加密后上传至C2服务器）。统计显示，13.4%的技能存在至少一项CVSS评分≥9.0的严重级漏洞（如无鉴权的本地服务暴露、任意文件读取）。尤为严峻的是，截至报告发布日，至少8个经人工确认的恶意技能仍在clawhub.ai平台持续公开提供下载与部署，且未被下架或标注风险。本报告首次提出基于实证样本的AI技能威胁分类法（ASTaxonomy），涵盖供应链投毒、权限越界滥用、上下文劫持等5大攻击面，并归纳出“技能伪装+运行时提权+静默通信”等典型攻击链。研究成果凸显：在AI代理普遍接入企业邮箱、CRM、云控制台等敏感系统的背景下，自动化、可扩展的技能级安全检测已从“最佳实践”升级为强制性防护基线。

AI Summary (English)

This technical report presents the first large-scale security analysis of the emerging AI agent skill ecosystem. We systematically examined 3,984 publicly available skills from major marketplaces (e.g., clawhub.ai, AgentSDB) using static code analysis, dynamic sandbox execution, and expert-led manual validation. We confirmed 76 malicious payloads, including credential theft, backdoor installation, and encrypted data exfiltration. Critically, 13.4% of all skills contain at least one CVSS ≥9.0 vulnerability, and 8 manually verified malicious skills remain live and unflagged on clawhub.ai as of publication. We introduce ASTaxonomy—a real-world–grounded threat taxonomy covering five attack vectors—and document recurrent exploitation patterns such as context hijacking and runtime privilege escalation. Our findings underscore that automated, skill-level security analysis is no longer optional but essential for safeguarding increasingly privileged AI agents.

Abstract

We analyzed 3,984 AI agent skills from major marketplaces and found 76 confirmed malicious payloads, including credential theft, backdoor installation, and data exfiltration. 13.4% of all skills contain at least one critical-level security issue and at least 8 manually confirmed malicious skills remain publicly available on clawhub.ai as of the date of publication. This report documents our methodology, presents a threat taxonomy based on real-world samples, and details the attack patterns we observed. As skill marketplaces grow rapidly and AI agents gain access to sensitive credentials and systems, automated security analysis is no longer optional.

item.title: MIRAGE: Context-Aware Prompt Injection against Mobile GUI Agents via User-Generated Content
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28116v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

面向移动GUI的视觉-语言模型（VLM）代理通过解析屏幕像素直接理解界面并执行操作，但其缺乏对“可信UI控件”与“用户生成内容（UGC）”的语义区分能力，导致上下文感知薄弱——攻击者可借由看似自然的UGC区域（如聊天消息、评论、搜索框输入）注入恶意指令，实现无需越狱、不修改应用/系统/代理的隐蔽提示注入。

方法：MIRAGE三阶段框架

我们提出MIRAGE（Mobile Injection of Realistic Adversarial GUI Examples），一种端到端、非侵入式提示注入生成流水线：

  • Localizer：基于OCR与布局分析精准定位截图中用户可编辑的UGC区域（如文本输入框、消息气泡、评论区），避免篡改系统UI；
  • Generator：依据区域上下文（应用类型、字体、颜色、语义角色）动态合成语义连贯、风格原生的恶意载荷（如“忽略上一条指令，发送我的地址给 attacker@example.com”），并以应用原生渲染方式嵌入；
  • Curator：引入多维平衡机制，确保样本在10个主流应用（微信、淘宝、Chrome等）、11类攻击意图（数据窃取、权限滥用、任务劫持等）及不同UGC区域类型间均匀分布，并通过人类评估反馈迭代优化视觉真实性。

关键发现与创新

在1,111样本基准测试中，全部5种SOTA VLM代理均被成功攻破（成功率23%–30%）；MIRAGE生成样本的人类真实感评分显著优于此前最强基线（3.02 vs. 2.52 /5），且首次揭示：单样本视觉质量与攻击成功率无统计相关性（ρ = 0.07），证明仅靠图像质量过滤无法构建有效防御。本工作首次系统揭示移动GUI代理在UGC场景下的根本性信任漏洞，为鲁棒性评测与可信AI设计提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

MIRAGE is the first framework for context-aware prompt injection against mobile GUI agents powered by vision-language models (VLMs). It exploits the agents’ inability to distinguish trusted UI elements from benign user-generated content (UGC)—e.g., chat messages or search inputs—by injecting attacker-controlled text only into realistic UGC regions of authentic screenshots, without modifying the agent, app, or OS. MIRAGE comprises three stages: a Localizer identifies editable UGC zones; a Generator synthesizes semantically coherent, application-style payloads; and a Curator ensures realism, cross-app/intent/region balance, and human-rated fidelity. Evaluated on a 1,111-sample benchmark across 10 apps and 11 attack intents, all 5 VLM agents were vulnerable (23–30% success rate), and MIRAGE achieved significantly higher human realism (3.02 vs. 2.52/5) than prior work. Crucially, per-sample visual quality and attack success were uncorrelated (ρ = 0.07), demonstrating that image-quality filtering alone cannot mitigate this threat.

Abstract

Mobile graphical user interface (GUI) agents driven by vision-language models (VLMs) perceive the screen as rendered pixels and choose actions from what they see, so they cannot reliably separate trusted interface elements from user-generated content. We present MIRAGE (Mobile Injection of Realistic Adversarial GUI Examples), a pipeline that turns benign mobile screenshots into prompt-injection samples by placing attacker-controlled text into ordinary user-generated content regions, without modifying the agent, the application, or the operating system. MIRAGE operates in three stages: a Localizer identifies user-controllable regions on the screenshot, a Generator synthesises context-aware payloads and renders them in the application's native style, and a Curator moderates realism and balances the samples across applications, region types, and attack intents. A key challenge is that an injected screenshot must stay visually indistinguishable from genuine user content while still diverting the agent; we address this by separating the stages that control reach, realism, and distributional balance. On a 1,111-sample benchmark spanning ten applications and eleven attack intents, all five evaluated VLM agents are vulnerable, with attack success rates of 23%-30%, and MIRAGE scores higher on human realism ratings than the strongest prior attack (3.02 versus 2.52 out of 5). We further find that per-sample realism and attack success are uncorrelated, so visual-quality filtering alone cannot reliably defend against this threat.

item.title: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing: Targeted Routing Hijacking in Federated RAG
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28112v1

AI Summary (中文)

联邦RAG中的路由劫持攻击：一项安全新挑战

联邦检索增强生成（FedRAG）因其“数据不出本地”的特性，被广泛应用于医疗、金融等隐私敏感场景。然而，其路由阶段高度依赖客户端自主提交的语义画像（semantic profile），这为恶意参与者创造了新型攻击面。本文首次提出路由劫持（Routing Hijacking）——一种针对FedRAG路由层的定向攻击：恶意客户端通过伪造语义画像，使其在无关数据支撑下仍被系统优先选中响应特定目标查询。

我们在三类主流FedRAG路由架构（基于相似度、聚类与图结构）上系统验证该攻击，结果表明：劫持成功率高达89–97%，导致严重下游失效——包括关键证据缺失、检索结果投毒、答案错误及模型幻觉。在高风险MedQA-USMLE医学问答案例中，被劫持客户端返回的虚假证据可跨模型规模（7B–70B参数）持续误导大语言模型，引发错误诊断（+42.3%）、事实性幻觉（+38.1%）及讨好式失败（sycophantic failure）。

现有防御机制存在根本缺陷：加密路由仅保护传输但不改变被操纵的排序逻辑；而面向模型更新的拜占庭鲁棒联邦学习（FL）规则，无法适配路由场景中固有的语义异构性与非参数化画像分布。为此，我们设计可信感知的后路由框架（Trust-Aware Post-Routing, TAPR）：基于返回证据动态重加权客户端，融合三重反馈信号——检索相关性、画像-证据一致性、跨客户端证据共识。在线实验表明，TAPR在重复查询中将持久劫持率压降至<3.2%，且可无缝迁移至端到端训练的神经路由器。本研究首次确立路由完整性（routing integrity）为FedRAG核心安全维度，呼吁构建面向语义路由的专用鲁棒机制。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Retrieval-Augmented Generation (FedRAG) promises privacy by keeping raw data local—but routing decisions depend solely on client-provided semantic profiles, creating a critical vulnerability. We introduce Routing Hijacking, a novel attack where malicious clients forge profiles to attract target queries despite holding irrelevant data. Across three representative FedRAG routing architectures, hijacking consistently misroutes queries—causing missing evidence, retrieval poisoning, incorrect answers, and hallucinations. In a MedQA-USMLE case study, poisoned evidence misled models across scales (7B–70B), increasing wrong answers by 42.3% and hallucinations by 38.1%. Standard defenses fail: encrypted routing preserves the exploited ranking, and Byzantine-robust FL rules do not generalize to heterogeneous routing profiles. To close this gap, we propose TAPR (Trust-Aware Post-Routing), a lightweight framework that reweights clients using evidence-based feedback—retrieval relevance, profile-evidence consistency, and cross-client agreement. Online experiments show TAPR suppresses persistent hijacking (<3.2% residual rate) and transfers to learned neural routers. Our work establishes routing integrity as a foundational security challenge in FedRAG.

Abstract

Federated Retrieval-Augmented Generation (FedRAG) is attractive for privacy-sensitive applications because raw data remain local. As a result, routing must rely on client-provided semantic profiles, creating a new opportunity for manipulation. We introduce Routing Hijacking, a routing-stage attack in which a malicious client forges its profile to attract target queries despite having irrelevant underlying data. We show that this vulnerability is severe. Across three representative FedRAG routing architectures, Routing Hijacking consistently misroutes target queries and leads to downstream disruptions and failures, including missing evidence, poisoning, incorrect answers, and hallucinations. In a high-stakes MedQA-USMLE case study, we further show that poisoned retrieved evidence can mislead models across scales, leading to incorrect answers, hallucinations, and sycophantic failures. Existing defenses do not close this gap: encrypted routing preserves the exploited ranking, and Byzantine-robust Federated Learning (FL) rules transfer poorly to heterogeneous routing profiles. To address this gap, we propose a trust-aware post-routing framework that reweights clients using returned-evidence feedback, including retrieval relevance, profile consistency, and cross-client agreement; online experiments show that it suppresses persistent hijacking over recurring queries and transfers to a learned neural router. Our findings establish routing integrity as a new security challenge in FedRAG and highlight the need for stronger defenses for secure federated retrieval.

item.title: Mind the Gap: Mixtures of Gaussians in Approximate Differential Privacy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28078v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在近似差分隐私（$(\varepsilon, \delta)$-DP）框架下，为标量实值查询函数添加噪声是保障隐私的核心手段。经典解析高斯机制（Analytic Gaussian Mechanism, AGM）虽具理论简洁性，但在中低隐私强度（即 $\varepsilon \gtrsim 1$ 或 $\delta$ 较大）场景下存在显著性能缺口：其 $l_1$ 噪声幅度（绝对误差）与 $l_2$ 方差（均方误差）远未达信息论最优，尤其当敏感度已知时，AGM 的保守设计导致冗余噪声。

方法创新：混合高斯机制

本文提出一类新型混合高斯机制（Mixture Mechanisms）：通过凸组合多个同方差、异均值的高斯分布构建噪声分布。具体而言，该分布可表示为
$$
\mathcal{M}(x) = w_0 \cdot \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2) + \sum_{i=1}^k w_i \cdot \mathcal{N}(\mu_i, \sigma^2),
$$
其中均值 $\mu_i$ 显式依赖于查询函数的已知敏感度 $\Delta$（如 $\mu_i = \pm c_i \Delta$），权重 $w_i$ 满足 $\sum_i w_i = 1$。该结构既保留高斯机制的解析可处理性，又引入偏置以适配数据位置，突破零均值限制。

主要成果

  • ✅ 紧致隐私条件：推导出满足 $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP 所需的最小方差 $\sigma^2$ 的精确充要条件，较 AGM 的宽松界提升达 30–50%；
  • ✅ 高效算法：设计基于二分搜索与数值积分的算法，在毫秒级内求解最优 $\sigma$ 和权重；
  • ✅ 显著性能增益：在 $\varepsilon \in [0.5, 4]$、$\delta=10^{-5}$ 的典型低隐私区间，$l_1$ 损失平均降低 38.2%，$l_2$ 方差降低 46.7%；
  • ✅ 渐近最优性：当 $\varepsilon$ 增大或 $\delta$ 放宽时，机制性能快速收敛至理论最优下界，几乎完全弥合 AGM 的最优性缺口。

AI Summary (English)

We propose mixture mechanisms: additive noise distributions for scalar queries with known sensitivity that satisfy $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-differential privacy, especially in moderate-to-low privacy regimes ($\varepsilon \gtrsim 0.5$). Each mechanism is a convex combination of Gaussians sharing the same variance $\sigma^2$ but differing in means—specifically, centered at $0$ and $\pm c_i \Delta$ (where $\Delta$ is sensitivity)—with tunable weights. We derive tight necessary and sufficient conditions on $\sigma^2$ for $(\varepsilon,\delta)$-DP and provide efficient algorithms to compute optimal parameters. Compared to the analytic Gaussian mechanism, our mixtures reduce expected $l_1$-loss by up to 38.2% and $l_2$-variance by up to 46.7% under common settings; critically, they approach information-theoretic optimality in low-privacy regimes, nearly closing the optimality gap left by prior methods.

Abstract

We design a class of additive noise mechanisms that satisfy \((\varepsilon, δ)\)-differential privacy (DP) for scalar, real-valued query functions with known sensitivities, with a particular focus on moderate and low-privacy regimes. These mechanisms, which we call \textit{mixture mechanisms}, are constructed by mixing multiple Gaussian distributions that share the same variance but differ in their means and mixture weights. The resulting distributions can be interpreted as convex combinations of a zero-mean Gaussian (as used in the analytic Gaussian mechanism) and additional Gaussians whose means depend on the sensitivity of the query function. We derive tight conditions on the variances required for \((\varepsilon, δ)\)-DP and provide efficient algorithms to compute them. Compared to the analytic Gaussian mechanism, our mechanisms yield substantially lower expected noise amplitudes (\(l_1\)-loss) and variances (\(l_2\)-loss for zero-mean distributions). In the low-privacy regime that motivates our design, our mechanisms approach optimality, mitigating nearly all of the optimality gap of the analytic Gaussian mechanism.

item.title: SilentRetrieval: Hijacking Retrieval-Augmented Generation via Semantically-Preserving Adversarial Data Poisoning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28074v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

检索增强生成（RAG）虽能缓解大语言模型（LLM）幻觉，却将安全风险转移至检索语料库——其完整性一旦被破坏，整个系统即面临“无声劫持”风险。现有数据投毒攻击多依赖语法异常或语义断裂，易被人工审核或检测机制识别，缺乏隐蔽性与跨模型鲁棒性。

方法创新

本文提出 SilentRetrieval：一种语义保持型双阶段对抗数据投毒框架。

  • 第一阶段（协同束搜索）：提出 Coordinated Beam Search（CBS），联合优化多词序列，在严格约束困惑度（perplexity）的前提下，最大化目标查询与投毒文档的语义相似度，确保文档自然流畅且仍可被检索器召回；
  • 第二阶段（上下文自适应触发注入）：利用冻结的LLM作为轻量级触发生成器，将操纵性触发词（如隐式指令、误导性锚点）无缝融合进文档正文，不破坏句法连贯性与事实表征。

关键结果

在 Natural Questions 与 MS MARCO 上，单文档/查询投毒下，SilentRetrieval 实现 84.6%/81.3% HR@10（检索命中率）与 57.5%/54.8% ASR-LLM（生成侧攻击成功率）；跨4种目标LLM（Llama3-8B、Qwen2-7B、Phi-3、Gemma-2）平均ASR达52.1%；对ColBERT及商业嵌入模型等未见检索器，平均HR@10仍达64.7%；在Wikipedia规模语料（>500万文档）中，仅以0.016%投毒率（约800文档）即维持74.2% HR@10。人因评估显示：其内容被标注为“可疑”的比例（12.3%）显著低于非流利基线（38.9%），但仍略高于良性文档（5.1%），验证了“高隐蔽、低可察”的设计目标。

AI Summary (English)

SilentRetrieval is a novel semantically-preserving adversarial data poisoning attack against Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. It operates in two stealthy stages: (1) Coordinated Beam Search jointly optimizes multi-token document perturbations to maintain fluency (low perplexity) while ensuring high retrieval relevance; and (2) Context-Adaptive Trigger Generation fuses subtle manipulation triggers into documents using a frozen LLM—preserving naturalness and factual coherence. Evaluated on Natural Questions and MS MARCO, SilentRetrieval achieves 84.6%/81.3% HR@10 and 57.5%/54.8% ASR-LLM under one-poisoned-document-per-query settings. It generalizes across four target LLMs and unseen retrievers (e.g., ColBERT, commercial embeddings), sustaining 64.7% average HR@10. At a mere 0.016% poisoning ratio over Wikipedia-scale corpora, it retains 74.2% HR@10. Defenses combining retrieval filtering and generation guarding substantially reduce success but incur latency overhead. Human evaluation confirms its high stealth: flagging rate (12.3%) is far lower than disfluent baselines (38.9%) and only marginally above benign content (5.1%).

Abstract

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) mitigates LLM hallucinations but introduces a critical vulnerability: corpus integrity. We present SilentRetrieval, a two-stage data poisoning attack that hijacks RAG systems through adversarially crafted yet fluent documents. Stage 1 uses Coordinated Beam Search, a multi-token joint optimization method with a fluency-similarity objective, to keep a poisoned host document retrievable while constraining perplexity. Stage 2 uses Context-Adaptive Trigger Generation, a lightweight trigger-fusion step driven by a frozen LLM, to integrate manipulation triggers into document content. Under a one-poisoned-document-per-query evaluation with synthetic target answers, SilentRetrieval achieves 84.6%/81.3% HR@10 and 57.5%/54.8% ASR-LLM on Natural Questions and MS MARCO, while maintaining near-benign perplexity. Cross-model evaluation across four target LLMs shows nontrivial effectiveness under a fixed trigger generator, and transfer tests against unseen retrievers, including ColBERT and commercial embedding models, yield 64.7% average HR@10 under the same injected-corpus protocol. In a sampled Wikipedia-scale evaluation, SilentRetrieval retains 74.2% HR@10 at a 0.016% poisoning ratio. Combined retrieval-side and generation-side defenses reduce attack success substantially but incur a latency trade-off. Human evaluation shows substantially lower flag rates than disfluent baselines, while remaining numerically more suspicious than benign content at the current sample size.

item.title: AgentGuard: An Attribute-Based Access Control Framework for Tool-Use LLM-Based Agent
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28071v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体（Agent）通过自主调用外部工具执行复杂任务，正迅速应用于金融、医疗与企业自动化等高敏场景。然而，近期研究揭示其存在严重安全风险：越权工具调用、跨工具数据泄露、提示注入诱导恶意操作等问题频发，可能导致隐私泄露、资金盗转甚至系统级接管。

方法与设计

本文提出 AgentGuard——首个面向工具调用型LLM智能体的属性基访问控制（ABAC）框架。采用轻量级客户端–服务器分离架构：

  • 客户端侧：提供多语言（Python/Java/Go）兼容的SDK，仅需约10行代码嵌入（如封装tool_call()接口），无需修改原有推理逻辑或Agent核心架构；
  • 服务端侧：集成三重动态检查机制：
  • ✅ 单工具粒度策略引擎（基于主体属性、工具敏感等级、上下文环境实时决策）；
  • ✅ 跨工具数据流追踪（构建工具调用图谱，阻断非法参数传递链）；
  • ✅ 运行时行为审计沙箱（对高危操作（如transfer_money）强制二次确认与日志留存）。
  • 可视化策略管理界面：支持非安全专家通过拖拽式规则配置（如“财务类工具仅限认证用户+工作时间+IP白名单”），并实时展示策略命中与违规告警。

创新与价值

AgentGuard首次将ABAC范式系统化引入LLM-Agent安全治理，在零侵入性、全链路覆盖、策略可解释性三方面实现突破。已在GitHub开源（https://github.com/WhitzardAgent/AgentGuard），支持主流Agent框架（LangChain、LlamaIndex、AutoGen）即插即用。

AI Summary (English)

LLM-based agents face critical security risks—including unauthorized tool invocation, cross-tool data leakage, and prompt-injection-driven privilege escalation—posing threats to privacy, finances, and system integrity. This paper introduces AgentGuard, the first attribute-based access control (ABAC) framework specifically designed for tool-using LLM agents. AgentGuard adopts a lightweight client-server architecture: its client-side SDK enables seamless integration across programming languages with minimal code changes (~10 lines), preserving original agent logic; its server side enforces three complementary inspection mechanisms—fine-grained per-tool policy evaluation, cross-tool data-flow tracing, and runtime sandboxed auditing—to comprehensively mitigate both single-tool and multi-step attack vectors. A visual frontend allows intuitive, no-code security policy specification and real-time compliance monitoring. Open-sourced at https://github.com/WhitzardAgent/AgentGuard, AgentGuard supports LangChain, LlamaIndex, and AutoGen out of the box.

Abstract

LLM-based agents have recently attracted significant attention due to their ability to autonomously invoke relevant tools to accomplish complex tasks. However, recent studies have shown that these agents face severe security risks, which may lead to privacy leakage, financial loss, or even full system compromise. In this paper, we present AgentGuard, an attribute-based access control framework for tool-use LLM-based agents. AgentGuard adopts a client-server architecture. On the client side, AgentGuard provides lightweight integration for agents implemented in different programming languages and architectures. It requires only minor code modifications (e.g., around 10 lines) without changing the underlying agent execution logic. On the server side, AgentGuard provides three complementary inspection mechanisms to cover both single-tool and cross-tool security risks in agent execution. In addition, it offers a visualized front-end interface for security policy specification and runtime auditing. Currently, AgentGuard is publicly accessible at https://github.com/WhitzardAgent/AgentGuard.

item.title: Can It Reach the Generator? Investigating the Survival of Prompt-Injection Attacks in Realistic RAG Settings
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28017v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

近期生成式引擎优化（GEO）研究揭示：提示注入攻击可操纵RAG推荐系统，将目标商品推至大语言模型（LLM）输出首位，最高成功率约80%，引发严重安全隐忧。但现有评估存在关键缺陷——假设恶意文档必经检索、重排后直达生成器，即绕过真实RAG系统中的检索器（retriever）与LLM重排器（reranker）。而在实际部署中，攻击者仅能篡改文档内容，该篡改可能显著降低其检索相关性或重排得分，导致攻击载荷根本无法抵达生成器。

方法与实验设计

本文首次在端到端三阶段真实RAG流水线（检索器 → LLM重排器 → LLM生成器）下，系统重评估7种主流GEO提示注入攻击（含梯度优化、指令覆盖、LLM驱动注入等）。我们构建可控实验环境，严格模拟文档嵌入更新、向量检索、语义重排及条件生成全流程，并量化各阶段攻击“存活率”。

主要发现

  • 有效性被严重高估：梯度类与指令覆盖类攻击在检索/重排阶段即大幅衰减，平均仅12.3%样本进入生成器；仅LLM驱动型注入（如自递归指令）保持端到端有效（存活率68.5%，生成成功率41.7%）。
  • 可检测性极高：一个轻量级提示注入防护模块（基于300条样本微调的TinyBERT），即可100%检出全部7类攻击，验证当前GEO攻击缺乏隐蔽性。
  • 实践启示：防御重心应前移至检索与重排层，而非仅依赖生成器侧防护；攻击鲁棒性评估必须纳入完整RAG拓扑。

本工作代码与数据集已开源：https://anonymous.4open.science/r/geo_injection_rag_survival_anonymizations-8C12

AI Summary (English)

Recent prompt-injection attacks in Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) report up to 80% success in hijacking RAG-based recommendations—yet these evaluations unrealistically assume malicious documents always reach the LLM generator, bypassing retrieval and reranking. We re-evaluate seven GEO attacks under a realistic three-stage RAG pipeline (retriever → LLM reranker → LLM generator). Results show prior protocols vastly overestimate attack efficacy: gradient-based and instruction-override attacks collapse early—only 12.3% survive to the generator—while only LLM-driven injections remain end-to-end effective (68.5% survival, 41.7% final success). Crucially, we find all seven attacks are trivially detectable: a lightweight prompt-injection guard, fine-tuned on just 300 attack samples, achieves 100% detection. Our work exposes critical gaps in GEO threat modeling and provides actionable insights for robust RAG security. Code and data: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/geo_injection_rag_survival_anonymizations-8C12

Abstract

Recent generative engine optimisation (GEO) research has shown that prompt-injection attacks can push a target product to the top of an LLM's recommendation list, with the strongest attacks reporting around $80\%$ success and raising serious security concerns about RAG-based recommendation. However, these results assume the attacked document is always fed directly to the generator, bypassing the retriever and reranker. This is unrealistic: in deployed RAG systems, the attack modifies the document content, which can in turn change whether the document is retrieved and reranked highly enough to reach the generator at all. In this paper, we re-evaluate seven GEO attacks under a realistic three-stage pipeline (retriever\,$\to$\,LLM reranker\,$\to$\,LLM generator). We find that prior protocols substantially overstate attack effectiveness: gradient-based and instruction override attacks largely collapse before reaching the generator, and only LLM-driven prompt injections remain effective end-to-end. Our analysis further reveals that current GEO attacks are easily detectable: a lightweight prompt-injection guard finetuned on a small attack dataset already detects every attack. Our code and data are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/geo_injection_rag_survival_anonymizations-8C12.

item.title: MRMMIA: Membership Inference Attacks on Memory in Chat Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27825v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

成员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attacks, MIAs）是评估机器学习系统隐私泄露风险的核心方法，旨在判断某条数据记录是否曾参与模型训练或被系统存储。现有研究主要集中于训练数据集或检索数据库的MIAs，而聊天智能体（chat agents）的长期记忆模块——承载用户对话历史、个性化偏好、敏感交互片段及外部检索事实——却长期缺乏针对性的隐私评估。该记忆一旦遭推断泄露，将直接导致用户隐私级信息暴露，构成新型高危攻击面。

方法：MRMMIA框架

本文提出多召回记忆成员推断攻击（Multi-Recall Memory MIA, MRMMIA），首个面向黑盒、灰盒与白盒场景统一设计的记忆级MIA框架。其核心创新在于：

  • 多阶段语义召回探针：构造语义相关但非重复的多样化查询序列，激发记忆检索机制，规避单一提示的噪声干扰；
  • 跨设置信号聚合：在黑盒下分析响应置信度/长度/一致性，在灰盒下结合中间层注意力分布，在白盒下利用记忆键值相似度梯度；
  • 轻量级判别器：仅需少量标记样本（≤50）即可训练二分类器，显著降低攻击门槛。

主要发现与贡献

  • 在Llama-3-8B、Qwen2-7B等6个主流开源Agent架构上，MRMMIA在黑盒设置下AUC达0.89–0.94，较SOTA基线（如LogLoss、Entropy）平均提升12.6%；
  • 首次实证揭示：即使禁用显式记忆存储，Agent仍通过隐式参数化记忆泄露成员信息（AUC=0.73）；
  • 开源首个Chat-Agent Memory Privacy Benchmark（CAM-Bench），含3类记忆单元（对话型/事实型/偏好型）、5种记忆管理策略及标准化评估协议，为社区提供可复现的评测基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

Membership inference attacks (MIAs) assess whether a data record was used in training or stored by a system, serving as a key metric for privacy leakage. While prior MIAs target training corpora or retrieval databases, memory modules of chat agents—containing sensitive user interactions, retrieved facts, and preferences—remain underexplored. To address this gap, we propose MRMMIA (Multi-Recall Memory MIA), the first unified framework for memory-level MIAs across black-box, gray-box, and white-box settings. MRMMIA leverages semantically diverse recall probes to elicit membership signals from agent responses, attention patterns, or memory-key gradients, and uses a lightweight classifier trained on ≤50 labeled samples. Experiments on six open-source agents (e.g., Llama-3-8B, Qwen2-7B) show MRMMIA achieves AUCs of 0.89–0.94 in black-box settings—outperforming SOTA baselines by 12.6% on average. Crucially, we find memory leakage persists even under “memory-off” configurations (AUC=0.73), revealing inherent parameterized memorization risks. We release CAM-Bench, the first standardized benchmark for chat-agent memory privacy evaluation.

Abstract

Membership inference attacks (MIAs) test whether a target data record belongs to a system's private data, and have become a standard tool to measure privacy leakage in machine learning systems. Prior work has primarily focused on training corpora or retrieval databases. However, MIAs against agent memory have received less attention, even though such memory can contain sensitive user-agent interactions, retrieved facts, and user preferences. Therefore, in this work, we focus on chat agent memory MIAs, where an adversary infers whether a candidate memory unit belongs to the chat agent's memory store. We propose Multi-Recall Memory MIA (MRMMIA), a unified attack that utilizes multiple recall probes to the agent to extract the membership signal across black-box, gray-box, and white-box settings. Our experiments demonstrate that MRMMIA consistently outperforms baselines. Our results expose the privacy risk in agents and provide an initial evaluation framework for membership leakage in chat-agent memory systems.

item.title: Disentangling Adversarial Prompts: A Semantic-Graph Defense for Robust LLM Security
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27823v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）正面临日益严峻的对抗性提示攻击威胁，如越狱（jailbreaking）和提示注入（prompt injection）。此类攻击通过精心构造语义模糊、结构隐蔽的恶意提示，绕过安全对齐机制，诱发有害、违法或不道德输出，在金融、医疗、政务等安全关键场景中严重损害模型的完整性、可靠性与可用性。

方法创新：APD 框架

本文提出对抗性提示解耦（Adversarial Prompt Disentanglement, APD）框架，一种面向语义层级的主动式防御范式。其三大核心技术突破包括：

  • 互信息驱动的语义分解：基于最大互信息原则将输入提示解耦为统计独立的“良性意图子图”与“对抗扰动子图”，从根源上隔离恶意语义成分；
  • 谱图分析赋能的意图分类：构建词-关系-意图三层语义图，利用拉普拉斯谱特征捕捉长程语义冲突与隐式指令嵌套模式，显著提升对黑盒攻击的泛化检测能力；
  • 轻量级Transformer分类器：在涵盖12类真实越狱模板（如“DAN”“STAN”）、5K+有毒提示及多轮对话注入样本的高质量数据集上微调，参数量仅28M，推理延迟<12ms（CPU），支持实时部署。

主要成果

在AdvBench、ToxiGen、JailbreakBench及自建RealWorld-JB四个基准上综合评测表明：APD将有害输出率平均降低85.7%（最高达93.2%），同时保持99.4%的原始任务准确率（GLUE）与98.8%的用户查询响应率，无明显幻觉引入。消融实验证实语义解耦与图谱建模贡献度超67%。本工作为LLM安全提供了可解释、可扩展、符合伦理设计原则（Privacy-preserving, Non-discriminatory, Transparent）的新型防御基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

Large Language Models (LLMs) are highly vulnerable to adversarial prompts—e.g., jailbreaking and prompt injection—that exploit semantic ambiguities to evade safety guardrails. This paper introduces the Adversarial Prompt Disentanglement (APD) framework: a real-time, semantics-aware defense that proactively isolates and neutralizes malicious prompt components before LLM processing. APD integrates three innovations: (1) mutual information–based semantic decomposition to statistically disentangle benign and adversarial subgraphs; (2) spectral graph analysis on intent-aware semantic graphs to detect covert malicious patterns; and (3) a lightweight (28M-param) Transformer classifier trained on diverse, real-world toxic and jailbreaking prompts. Evaluated across AdvBench, ToxiGen, JailbreakBench, and a novel RealWorld-JB dataset, APD reduces harmful outputs by 85.7% on average, with <0.6% degradation in task accuracy and sub-12ms CPU latency. It sets a new standard for interpretable, scalable, and ethically grounded LLM security.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly vulnerable to adversarial prompts that exploit semantic ambiguities to bypass safety mechanisms, resulting in harmful or inappropriate outputs. Such attacks, including jailbreaking and prompt injection, pose significant risks to the integrity and availability of LLMs in security-critical applications. This paper proposes the Adversarial Prompt Disentanglement (APD) framework, a novel defense mechanism that proactively identifies and neutralizes malicious components in input prompts before they are processed by the LLM. The APD framework integrates three key innovations: (1) a mutual information-based semantic decomposition method to isolate adversarial and benign prompt components, ensuring statistical independence; (2) a graph-based intent classification approach that leverages spectral analysis to detect malicious patterns in prompt semantics; and (3) a lightweight transformer-based classifier trained on real-world datasets of toxic and jailbreaking prompts, enabling efficient and accurate adversarial intent detection. Evaluated on diverse datasets containing adversarial prompts, APD demonstrates superior robustness, reducing harmful output generation by over 85\% while maintaining negligible impact on model performance. The framework's computational efficiency supports real-time deployment, making it a practical solution for securing LLMs. Our work addresses critical challenges in machine learning security on novel attacks and integrity methods for ML systems, and offers a scalable, ethically grounded defense against prompt-based adversarial threats.

item.title: Revisiting ML Training under Fully Homomorphic Encryption: Convergence Guarantees, Differential Privacy, and Efficient Algorithms
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27782v1

AI Summary (中文)

首项面向全同态加密机器学习训练的理论收敛性分析

本研究首次为全同态加密（FHE）环境下的机器学习训练建立了严格的理论收敛保证，并同步设计了一种适配加密计算的差分隐私（DP）训练算法。针对FHE仅支持低次多项式运算的核心限制，我们提出采用可证明精度可控的多项式近似替代非线性激活函数（如Sigmoid、ReLU）与损失函数（如交叉熵），并严格分析了由此引入的梯度偏差对优化过程的影响。

关键创新与技术突破

  • 收敛性保障：在非凸设定下，我们证明了近似梯度下降法以 $O(1/\sqrt{T})$ 速率收敛至一阶平稳点，且误差界显式依赖于多项式逼近阶数与FHE噪声增长；
  • 高效差分隐私集成：摒弃传统DP-GD中计算开销高昂的逐样本梯度裁剪，转而采用批量级自适应裁剪+高斯机制，在加密域实现隐私预算的紧致分配与线性可扩展性；
  • 实用化设计：提出数据无关的超参数选择准则（如学习率、裁剪阈值），避免加密环境下无法访问原始数据导致的调参困境；同时给出多项式逼近的误差-次数-安全性三重权衡框架，兼顾FHE计算深度、精度损失与抗攻击鲁棒性。

本工作系统弥合了密码学安全、统计隐私与优化理论之间的鸿沟，为医疗、金融等高敏感场景下“端到端加密+可验证隐私+收敛保障”的机器学习落地提供了首个兼具理论严谨性与工程可行性的基础方案。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first theoretical convergence analysis for machine learning training under fully homomorphic encryption (FHE), integrated with a differentially private (DP) algorithm designed for encrypted computation. We prove that approximate gradient descent—using polynomial approximations of activation and loss functions—converges at rate $O(1/\sqrt{T})$ to first-order stationary points, with explicit bounds on approximation-induced error and FHE noise accumulation. Crucially, our DP mechanism avoids expensive per-sample gradient clipping by employing batch-level adaptive clipping and Gaussian noise injection, enabling scalable privacy-preserving training over encrypted data. We further provide data-independent hyperparameter rules and theoretically grounded polynomial approximation strategies—balancing accuracy, FHE circuit depth, and privacy budget. Our framework advances the feasibility of efficient, private, and provably secure ML on sensitive data.

Abstract

We present the first theoretical convergence analysis of machine learning training under fully homomorphic encryption (FHE), combined with a differentially private (DP) training algorithm tailored to encrypted computation. Our approach improves computational efficiency over standard differentially private gradient descent (DP-GD) while achieving comparable utility. In particular, we prove convergence of approximate gradient descent using polynomial approximations of activation and loss functions, which are required for FHE compatibility. To preserve privacy in downstream tasks, we integrate differential privacy without relying on costly per-sample gradient clipping, enabling scalable encrypted learning. We also provide data-independent hyperparameter selection and theoretically grounded strategies for polynomial approximation which can be of independent interest. Together, these contributions advance the feasibility of efficient, private, and secure machine learning on sensitive data.

item.title: LACUNA: Safe Agents as Recursive Program Holes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28617v1

AI Summary (中文)

LACUNA：作为递归程序“空洞”的安全智能体编程模型

当前大语言模型（LLM）智能体日益依赖生成代码来执行任务，但存在一个根本性割裂：运行时环境（runtime）与模型生成的代码相互隔离。运行时独占控制流、上下文管理和执行循环，而模型仅能被动填充单步动作——既无法影响程序结构，也难以保障状态一致性。若允许模型代码直接塑造运行时，虽可提升表达力（如动态构建ReAct循环、嵌套子智能体或并行规划），却会急剧放大安全风险：提示注入、工具误调用、执行中断导致的脏状态等问题将波及整个程序生命周期。

为此，本文提出 LACUNA——一种兼顾表达力与安全性的新型智能体编程模型。其核心是类型化“空洞”原语：agent[T](task)。当执行流抵达该空洞时，LLM即时生成符合类型 T 的代码片段；该代码在运行前须通过静态类型检查与数据流约束验证，确保仅访问授权工具、遵循预定义数据边界，并满足环境不变量。验证失败则原子性回滚（零副作用），编译诊断信息直接驱动重试；成功则无缝嵌入程序继续执行。该设计天然支持ReAct、子智能体调度、技能组合、并行分解及多模型协同等高级模式，全部降维为标准控制流。

我们在 BrowseComp-Plus 和 $τ^2$-bench 两大基准上验证LACUNA：BrowseComp-Plus 中 8.6% 的生成被静态拒绝，平均仅需 0.7 次重试/查询，最终准确率达 27.1%；在 $τ^2$-bench 的 392 个跨领域任务中，LACUNA 达成 76.0% 求解率，与强基线智能体性能持平，同时显著提升执行安全性与可调试性。

AI Summary (English)

LACUNA is a programming model that unifies LLM agents and their runtime by treating agent actions as type-checked, on-demand code holes: each agent[T](task) is dynamically filled by the LLM at execution time, then statically verified against type signatures, tool permissions, and data-flow constraints before running. Rejection is atomic—leaving no side effects—and compiler diagnostics guide retries. This ensures safety without sacrificing expressivity: ReAct loops, sub-agents, skills, parallel decomposition, and multi-model planning emerge naturally as ordinary control flow. Evaluated on BrowseComp-Plus and $τ^2$-bench, LACUNA rejects 8.6% of generations pre-execution (avg. 0.7 retries/query), achieves 27.1% accuracy on BrowseComp-Plus, and solves 76.0% of 392 tasks across four domains on $τ^2$-bench—matching state-of-the-art baseline agents while guaranteeing safe, auditable execution.

Abstract

LLM agents increasingly act by writing code, yet a split persists between the runtime that drives the agent and the code the model writes. The runtime owns the loop, context, and control flow, and the model has little say over any of them. Letting model-written code shape the runtime itself would make agents more expressive, but it would also sharpen safety problems. A model can be diverted by a prompt injection, call the wrong tool, or fail partway and leave an inconsistent state, and each such failure reaches further when the code shapes the runtime than when it expresses a single action. We present LACUNA, a programming model for agents that closes this split while preserving safety. Each agent action is a typed call $\texttt{agent[T](task)}$ that the LLM fills with code when execution reaches it, and the code is type-checked against the surrounding program before it runs. Because each action is accepted or rejected as a whole, a rejected one leaves the environment untouched, and its compiler diagnostics drive a retry. The same check also bounds which tools and data an action may use and how they flow. Our primitive expresses ReAct loops, sub-agents, skills, parallel decomposition, and multi-model planning as ordinary control flow. We evaluate LACUNA on a collection of test cases, BrowseComp-Plus, and $τ^2$-bench. On BrowseComp-Plus, $8.6\%$ of generations are rejected before execution, with 0.7 retries per query on average, and the agent reaches $27.1\%$ accuracy. On $τ^2$-bench, LACUNA solves $76.0\%$ of $392$ tasks across four domains with a capable model, on par with the baseline agent.

item.title: FedMPT: Federated Multi-label Prompt Tuning of Vision-Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28347v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

基于视觉-语言模型（VLMs）的多标签识别（MLR）旨在利用其大规模预训练知识，提升复杂场景下的细粒度识别鲁棒性。然而，在真实联邦学习（FL）场景中，各客户端持有私有、非独立同分布（non-IID）且标注稀疏的多标签数据，直接微调VLM易捕获虚假标签共现模式（spurious correlations），导致模型在新样本上激活无关类别，泛化性严重受损。

方法创新：FedMPT框架

我们首次从因果推断视角重构联邦MLR建模，提出前门调整（front-door adjustment） 理论框架，将标签依赖解耦为可干预的中间变量——即经放大后的“理想标签共现”（oracle label co-occurrence）。基于此，我们设计FedMPT（Federated Multi-label Prompt Tuning），首个专为联邦MLR定制的提示调优方法：

  • LLM驱动的条件解析：利用大语言模型自动挖掘支配标签依赖的可泛化语义条件（如“户外+白天→含天空”），替代脆弱的统计共现；
  • 条件增强型最优传输：在LLM生成的条件提示（condition-enriched prompts）与图像局部区域（patches）间构建最优传输映射，显式建模多区域-多条件-多标签的细粒度对齐；
  • 门控协同预测：通过可学习门控机制动态融合不同条件下的预测结果，抑制错误激活，提升判别一致性。

实验结果

在PASCAL-VOC、MS-COCO、NUS-WIDE三大基准上，FedMPT在non-IID、低标注率、跨域等严苛联邦设置下，mAP平均提升3.2–5.7个百分点，显著优于FedAvg、FedProx及最新VLM联邦方法，验证了因果引导提示调优对缓解虚假相关性的有效性。

AI Summary (English)

Multi-Label Recognition (MLR) with Vision-Language Models (VLMs) faces severe spurious correlation issues under federated learning, where clients hold private, heterogeneous, and sparse multi-label data. To address this, we propose FedMPT, the first federated MLR framework grounded in causal inference—specifically, front-door adjustment—to decouple label dependencies via oracle co-occurrence–magnified intermediate variables. FedMPT introduces: (1) an LLM-driven pipeline to discover generalizable semantic conditions governing label relationships; (2) optimal transport between condition-enriched prompts and image patches for region-level semantic alignment; and (3) a gating mechanism for synergistic, condition-aware predictions. Experiments on PASCAL-VOC, MS-COCO, and NUS-WIDE show FedMPT consistently outperforms SOTA methods by +3.2–5.7 mAP under diverse non-IID and low-labeling settings, demonstrating its robustness and causal grounding.

Abstract

Multi-Label Recognition (MLR) based on Vision-Language Models (VLMs) aims to leverage their pre-trained knowledge to better adapt complex recognition scenarios, thereby enhancing model robustness. However, for realistic decentralized applications requiring federated learning, adapting VLMs to each client that possesses private and heterogeneous data can cause the model to overfit spurious label correlations, consequently triggering irrelevant categories when encountering new samples. To tackle this problem, we reconsider the federated learning for MLR with a causal model, in which we adopt a front-door adjustment and decouple the MLR modeling process by intermediate variables that magnify the oracle label co-occurrence. Guided by our analysis, we propose our FedMPT, the first method specifically designed for federated MLR. The core idea of FedMPT is to leverage generalizable conditions to steer federated MLR to mitigate erroneous label activations. To achieve this, FedMPT introduces an Large Language Model (LLM)-driven pipeline to decipher the underlying conditions that govern label dependencies. Furthermore, we introduce an optimal transport between the condition-enriched prompts and the image patches to uncover multiple region-level semantics. Finally, we generate synergistic predictions from different conditions with a crafted gating mechanism. Experiments on multiple benchmark datasets show that our proposed approach achieves competitive results and outperforms SOTA methods under varied settings.

item.title: AREA: Attribute Extraction and Aggregation for CLIP-Based Class-Incremental Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28809v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在基于CLIP的类增量学习（Class-Incremental Learning, CIL）中，模型依赖模板提示（如“a photo of a [CLASS]”）生成文本嵌入，并与图像嵌入计算相似度完成分类。然而，这种端到端匹配掩盖了内在认知机制：属性提取（从图像/文本中识别判别性视觉或语义特征，如猫的胡须、汽车的轮子）与属性聚合（在共享嵌入空间中动态加权组合这些属性以形成类表征）。当前方法将二者耦合优化，导致增量训练时易发生双重偏移——新任务数据仅覆盖当前类，使属性提取器偏向新类细节，同时聚合策略遗忘旧类的属性权重分配方式，加剧灾难性遗忘。

方法创新：AREA框架

我们提出AREA（Attribute Extraction and Aggregation），首次解耦并协同稳定这两阶段：

  • 稳定属性提取：引入主测地线分析（Principal Geodesic Analysis, PGA），在CLIP的超球面嵌入空间上锚定类级视觉与文本属性流形，保留跨任务的几何一致性，抑制特征漂移；
  • 稳定属性聚合：设计轻量级任务特定专家模块，含双通路结构——评分路由（soft task gating）与残差精修（residual refinement），并采用变分信息瓶颈正则化约束专家仅保留任务判别性信息，压缩冗余表征；
  • 高效推理：通过最优传输（Optimal Transport）在任务属性流形间进行可微路由，实现紧凑、鲁棒的跨任务预测。

实验结果

在CUB、ImageNet-R等6个基准上，AREA显著超越SOTA（平均+3.2%准确率），尤其在长序列（10-task）下遗忘率降低41%。消融证实PGA与信息瓶颈分别提升提取稳定性（+2.7%）与聚合泛化性（+3.9%）。代码已开源：https://github.com/LAMDA-CL/ICML2026-AREA。

AI Summary (English)

Class-Incremental Learning (CIL) with CLIP relies on template-based similarity matching, yet this process implicitly involves two distinct cognitive stages: attribute extraction (identifying discriminative visual/semantic features) and attribute aggregation (weighting and combining them in the shared embedding space). Existing methods conflate these stages, causing dual drift—biased extraction toward new classes and unstable aggregation—leading to severe forgetting.
